issue_title: [Bug] Multiple IPv6 MPREACH attributes in one UPDATE with group-updates true
issue_body:
Hi Thomas

I am using ExaBGP for a customer project and would like to express my gratitude at this point. ExaBGP does a great job when it comes to injecting routes into BGP. Thanks a lot for all your efforts!

**Observed behavior**
Recently, our customer started using IPv6 and injecting a first /128 route using ExaBGP. Everything worked well. Then the customer started to inject two more /128 routes with the same next-hop IPv6 address. Still, everything worked well.
Finally, the customer started setting different next-hops for each route and suddenly, the neighbor device kept learning just one route while simply ignoring the other two. I suspected this to be a bug in the firmware of the neighbor device and started digging.

**Long story short**
Setting "group-updates true" - which, according to the log will become the default in ExaBGP soon - leads to an incorrect update message sent by ExaBGP.
### ExaBGP Version

```
root@exabgp-test:~# exabgp --version
ExaBGP : 3.4.16
Python : 2.7.12 (default, Jul  1 2016, 15:12:24)  [GCC 5.4.0 20160609]
Uname  : #55-Ubuntu SMP Thu Aug 11 18:01:55 UTC 2016
```
### Example Config

```
group example {
 router-id <A.B.C.D>;
 local-as 65002;
 peer-as 65001;
 md5 ExamplePassword123;
 hold-time 180;
 group-updates true;

 neighbor <neighbor-ipv6-address> {
   description "IPv6 eBGP session with example";
   local-address <exabgp-ipv6-address>;

   family {
     ipv6 unicast;
   }

   static {
     route 2001:db8::1/128 next-hop <nexthop1-ipv6-address>;
     route 2001:db8::2/128 next-hop <nexthop2-ipv6-address>;
   }
 }
}
```
### With group-updates enabled (group-updates true)

```
IP6 (flowlabel 0x93575, hlim 64, next-header TCP (6) payload length: 156) <exabgp-ipv6-address>.45712 > <neighbor-ipv6-address>.179: Flags [P.], cksum 0xbdb4 (correct), seq 65:181, ack 65, win 225, options [nop,nop,...], length 116: BGP, length: 116
    Update Message (2), length: 116
      Origin (1), length: 1, Flags [T]: IGP
      AS Path (2), length: 4, Flags [T]: 65002
      Multi-Protocol Reach NLRI (14), length: 38, Flags [O]: 
        AFI: IPv6 (2), SAFI: Unicast (1)
        nexthop: <nexthop1-ipv6-address>, nh-length: 16, no SNPA
          2001:db8::1/128
      Multi-Protocol Reach NLRI (14), length: 38, Flags [O]: 
        AFI: IPv6 (2), SAFI: Unicast (1)
        nexthop: <nexthop2-ipv6-address>, nh-length: 16, no SNPA
          2001:db8::2/128
```
### With group-updates disabled (group-updates false)

```
IP6 (flowlabel 0xa3adf, hlim 64, next-header TCP (6) payload length: 115) <exabgp-ipv6-address>.44929 > <neighbor-ipv6-address>.179: Flags [P.], cksum 0x9436 (correct), seq 65:140, ack 65, win 225, options [nop,nop,...], length 75: BGP, length: 75
    Update Message (2), length: 75
      Origin (1), length: 1, Flags [T]: IGP
      AS Path (2), length: 4, Flags [T]: 65002
      Multi-Protocol Reach NLRI (14), length: 38, Flags [O]: 
        AFI: IPv6 (2), SAFI: Unicast (1)
        nexthop: <nexthop1-ipv6-address>, nh-length: 16, no SNPA
          2001:db8::1/128
[...]
IP6 (flowlabel 0xa3adf, hlim 64, next-header TCP (6) payload length: 115) <exabgp-ipv6-address>.44929 > <neighbor-ipv6-address>.179: Flags [P.], cksum 0x133e (correct), seq 140:215, ack 65, win 225, options [nop,nop,...], length 75: BGP, length: 75
    Update Message (2), length: 75
      Origin (1), length: 1, Flags [T]: IGP
      AS Path (2), length: 4, Flags [T]: 65002
      Multi-Protocol Reach NLRI (14), length: 38, Flags [O]: 
        AFI: IPv6 (2), SAFI: Unicast (1)
        nexthop: <nexthop2-ipv6-address>, nh-length: 16, no SNPA
          2001:db8::2/128
```
### Important RFCs

RFC 4271 (BGP-4), section 6.3 states:

> If any attribute appears more than once in the UPDATE message, then
> the Error Subcode MUST be set to Malformed Attribute List.

RFC 7606 (Revised Error Handling for BGP), section 3.g. states:

> If the MP_REACH_NLRI attribute or the MP_UNREACH_NLRI [RFC4760]
> attribute appears more than once in the UPDATE message, then a
> NOTIFICATION message MUST be sent with the Error Subcode
> "Malformed Attribute List".  If any other attribute (whether
> recognized or unrecognized) appears more than once in an UPDATE
> message, then all the occurrences of the attribute other than the
> first one SHALL be discarded and the UPDATE message will continue
> to be processed.
### Conclusion

It appears that both the neighbor device as well as ExaBGP are not entirely following the RFCs:
- ExaBGP should not have multiple MPREACH attributes in the same UPDATE message.
- The neighbor device should return a "Malformed Attribute List" Error instead of processing the update message and discarding (some of) the routes.

May I kindly ask you to take a quick look at this and let me know if you agree? I have advised the customer to set "group-updates false" for the time being, so we are in no hurry at all.

Let me know if you need anything else from me.

Cheers,
Manuel

issue_title: Support of VPLS Auto-Discovery messages in ExaBGP
issue_body:
RFC 4761 describe the use of BGP for L2VPN VPLS service.
There are 2 types of BGP messages for VPLS: Signaling (already implemented and functionnal :-)), and Auto-Discovery (RFC 4761 part 3.1).

In many provider networks, the signaling is handled by LDP while the VPLS membership discovery and announcement is done via BGP.

I can provide captures of BGP VPLS AD update if needed.
Regards,
Pierre

issue_title: support building against mingw headers
issue_body:
Building on MSYS2 requires some minor changes, please consider merging them:
- only include `gss.h` as the other header files don't exist - this can easily be IFDEFed (I just hacked it)
- `gss_mech_krb5` is not defined in those header files, I used python `struct.pack` to generate the value
- I provided environment variables to skip detection, alternatively the detection can be enhanced to detect the mingw paths
- skip running `krb5-config gssapi` which is not installed - it would error out

To test, just install [msys2](https://www.msys2.org/), then install gss: `pacman -S mingw-w64-x86_64-gss` and then try to build `python-gssapi`.

To see the exact changes that I made to get it to build, please see:
[xpra ticket 1691 - comment 7](http://xpra.org/trac/ticket/1691#comment:7)

issue_title: Drop py2.6 support
issue_body:
As the drop from `pytest` via pytest-dev/pytest#2812 we can now remove lingering code that is only in place to support old python.

See pytest-dev/pytest#2822 for comparable CI related changes that are likely appropriate. 

Some stuff I can think of off hand includes:
- set logic for [checking missing hookcall args](https://github.com/pytest-dev/pluggy/blob/master/pluggy/__init__.py#L672) can be modernized to use new `{,}` declarations and `dict.viewkeys()`.
- `str.format()` method now supports automatic numbering in py2.7+
- leveraging `collections.OrderedDict` to simplify some of the internal data structures for managing hooks

issue_title: Improve snippet startup procedure to avoid blind waits for connection
issue_body:
Currently, we start the snippet and blindly wait until it's available on the given port. This causes several problems:

    No way to know that a snippet launched (versus crashing immediately due to e.g. classpath issues, so we don't need to bother trying to connect to a dead snippet)
    No way to know what version of startup and communication protocol it's using so we can configure the client appropriately and manage compatibility.
    No way to allow the device side to choose a port number, to avoid the client side having to pick a port number for the snippet and hoping it's free.
    Hard to debug situations where snippet is hanging as we don't know how far it's gotten.

We need to fix the snippet startup procedure.

issue_title: Deprecate get_default_value method?
issue_body:
From PR #31 - the `get_default_value` method on traits now just returns `self.default_value` in all the traits we define. Dynamically created defaults (e.g. for `Instance` traitlets) are built via a different mechanism, so that we can avoid creating them until they're needed.

If there are no other traitlets defining or using `get_default_value`, we can deprecate it and just use `self.default_value` instead. Widgets [doesn't appear to](https://github.com/ipython/ipywidgets/blob/master/ipywidgets/widgets/trait_types.py). Are there any other users of widgets we know about?

CC @minrk @SylvainCorlay

issue_title: should `trait_names` return iterator or list? [P3 Compat]
issue_body:
`HasTraits.trait_names` and `HasTraits.class_own_trait_names` return a `dict_keys` iterator in Python 3, but a `list` in Python 2. Should this remain true, or should we be returning lists in both versions?

+ It would be in the style of Python 3 to keep the iterators
+ However, we could return lists for the sake of uniformity

As a side note, returning a `dict_keys` iterator prevents the actual dict object it refers to (which is never made available to the user) from being garbage collected. So if we want to keep iterators, that might be something to consider changing.

issue_title: speed bottleneck withing decode_mapi
issue_body:
Hello,

today i had a problem with a customer tnef mail. It tooks around 150ms to call `decode_mapi`. Because it is called several times within tnefparse and within my code, it took around 2500ms to handle a request to this file.

I searched the bottleneck with cProfile and found the problem:

In `decode_mapi` the var `num_vals` is a int bigger then several millions. Python will generate a list this big...
Solution: do not generate this big list, just use a iterator: xrange()

Speed change: 2500ms > 60ms

issue_title: When configured to use 'copy' file_action pulsar still attempts http transfer
issue_body:
Hi John - I emailed about this earlier, but did some more digging. I'm guessing my hacks on the code in the diffs below break things in other setups, but they are letting the 'copy' file_action complete.

Tried to configure pulsar with a destination as follows:

```xml
<destination id="pulsar_rest_dest" runner="pulsar_rest">
            <param id="url">http://localhost:3032/</param>
            <param id="private_token">xxxxxx</param>
            <param id="default_file_action">copy</param>
            <param id="submit_user">$__user_name__</param>
            <param id="submit_native_specification">--partition=super</param>
            <param id="file_action_config">/project/apps/galaxy/galaxy/config/pulsar_actions.yaml</param>
        </destination>
```
Job submission always failed. Found pulsar was trying to upload files via http, not copy them. Worked through call trace and found that action_type is being picked up correctly, but is not being passed into the client.put_file method. This method has a default action_type in it's def which means http transfer was always used.

Modified to pass the action_type through with diff:

```diff
--- a/lib/pulsar/client/staging/up.py
+++ b/lib/pulsar/client/staging/up.py
@@ -348,8 +348,7 @@ class TransferTracker(object):
         if action.staging_needed:
             local_action = action.staging_action_local
             if local_action:
-                response = self.client.put_file(path, type, name=name, contents=contents)
-
+                response = self.client.put_file(path, type, name=name, contents=contents, action_type=action.action_type)
                 def get_path():
                     return response['path']
             else:

```
It then attempted to copy files, but was failing in the shutil copy call as the path obtained from pulsar is a json structure, not a bare string path. abspath calls mangling it into a combination of the GALAXY_HOME and the json structure:

```
==> handler1.log <==
galaxy.jobs.runners.pulsar ERROR 2015-04-08 15:46:17,100 failure running job 83
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/project/apps/galaxy/galaxy/lib/galaxy/jobs/runners/pulsar.py", line 209, in queue_job
    job_id = pulsar_submit_job(client, client_job_description, remote_job_config)
  File "/project/apps/galaxy/galaxy/lib/pulsar/client/staging/up.py", line 22, in submit_job
    file_stager = FileStager(client, client_job_description, job_config)
  File "/project/apps/galaxy/galaxy/lib/pulsar/client/staging/up.py", line 91, in __init__
    self.__upload_input_files()
  File "/project/apps/galaxy/galaxy/lib/pulsar/client/staging/up.py", line 160, in __upload_input_files
    self.__upload_input_file(input_file)
  File "/project/apps/galaxy/galaxy/lib/pulsar/client/staging/up.py", line 166, in __upload_input_file
    self.transfer_tracker.handle_transfer(input_file, path_type.INPUT)
  File "/project/apps/galaxy/galaxy/lib/pulsar/client/staging/up.py", line 351, in handle_transfer
    response = self.client.put_file(path, type, name=name, contents=contents, action_type=action.action_type)
  File "/project/apps/galaxy/galaxy/lib/pulsar/client/client.py", line 177, in put_file
    copy(path, pulsar_path)
  File "/project/apps/galaxy/galaxy/lib/pulsar/client/util.py", line 27, in copy
    shutil.copyfile(source, destination)
  File "/project/apps/galaxy/.pyenv/versions/2.7.8/lib/python2.7/shutil.py", line 83, in copyfile
    with open(dst, 'wb') as fdst:
IOError: [Errno 2] No such file or directory: '/project/apps/galaxy/galaxy/{"path": "/project/apps/galaxy/pulsar/files/staging/d283a74499144b89ae73666463c2bc73/inputs/dataset_91.dat"}'
```

Tried to fix this with a naive patch:

```diff
--- a/lib/pulsar/client/client.py
+++ b/lib/pulsar/client/client.py
@@ -1,5 +1,5 @@
 import os
-from json import dumps
+from json import dumps, loads

 from .destination import submit_params
 from .setup_handler import build as build_setup_handler
@@ -173,7 +173,7 @@ class JobClient(BaseJobClient):
         if action_type == 'transfer':
             return self._upload_file(args, contents, input_path)
         elif action_type == 'copy':
-            pulsar_path = self._raw_execute('path', args)
+            pulsar_path = loads(self._raw_execute('path', args))['path']
             copy(path, pulsar_path)
             return {'path': pulsar_path}

```

It's now staging files by copying. I'm not sure how much other stuff these diffs are likely to break though.

issue_title: Cryptography Libraries
issue_body:
As currently structured this project has dependencies on both PyCrypto and M2Crypto. This doesn't seem ideal, especially as the latter is essentially a moribund project with no active path towards py3 compatibility. Is there a plan to move to just one?

Full disclosure: This came to my attention because I work on [pyca/cryptography](https://github.com/pyca/cryptography), which is the underlying dependency for [pyOpenSSL](https://github.com/pyca/pyopenssl) and the [future of PyCrypto](https://lists.dlitz.net/pipermail/pycrypto/2014q3/000814.html). pyca/cryptography itself currently would not fit your needs as our X509 support is incomplete (as is our serialization of asymmetric keys), but we're working on it.

issue_title: OBJ Exporter Segfaults due to vertex colours
issue_body: In https://github.com/assimp/assimp/blob/master/code/ObjExporter.cpp#L249, the vector of vertex colours is assumed to be the same length as the vector of positions, but since they're indexed separately, this may not be the case, leading to a potential out of bounds access of the vector of colours.

issue_title: Embedded texture loading inconsistency between FBX and GLTF2
issue_body:
I noticed this while digging around texture loading behaviour.

Currently FBX will store embedded textures by their file path, while GLTF 2 (possibly others too, haven't checked) will store them with an asterisk and an index (e.g. `*2`). As far as I know, GLTF 2 has in some cases no other choice, as textures not necessarily need to have a name.

When loading mixed file types, this leads to inconsistent handling of embedded textures.
My proposal: Let GetEmbeddedTexture from aiScene also load embedded textures with an asterisk. (see loebl/assimp@e217358)
An alternative would be to set this texture reference also as file name, so GetEmbeddedTexture does not need to be changed.

issue_title: Support datapackages as data sources
issue_body:
[Implementation suggestion]

**Detection** - one of:
- `format` == `datapackage`
- `basename(url)` == `datapackage.json`

**Resolution**:
When `format` is `datapackage`, `url` is resolved according to [data-package-identifier spec](http://specs.frictionlessdata.io/data-package-identifier/#identifier-object-structure)

**Resource selection**:
- An optional `resource` parameter.
  - If numeric: specifies the index of the resource
  - If string: specifies the `name` of the resource (first one that matches)
  - If unspecified: takes the 1st resource

**Resource handling**:
- If *Tabular* data package - iterates on the resource itself, using the schema
- If *Non-Tabular* datapackage - resolves to the file referenced by the resource, and continues regular handling from there.

issue_title: Speech client incorrectly computes chunk sizes
issue_body:
This code reads chunk_size bytes from the audio file instead of reading chunk_size * encoding_frame_size bytes:
[https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/master/speech/google/cloud/speech/_gax.py#L348][1]

This seems like  a bug, since the documentation for chunk_size  implies that it is trying to achieve 100 ms chunks:
[https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/master/speech/google/cloud/speech/sample.py#L78][2]

In a typical LINEAR16 encoding, this means that we would actually be reading 50 ms chunks instead of 100.

[1]: https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/39feb3e23d451963ca31b383d8b395afcf7ab817/speech/google/cloud/speech/_gax.py#L348
[2]: https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/39feb3e23d451963ca31b383d8b395afcf7ab817/speech/google/cloud/speech/sample.py#L78

issue_body:
Thus any inclusion of its header files should use `#include <json.h>` as opposed to `#include <json-c/json.h>`, which breaks on systems that rely on `pkg-config` to detect include dir and link dir for libraries.

From json-c upstream: https://github.com/json-c/json-c/blob/master/Makefile.am#L17

This is true even on debian-9.
```

...
# pkg-config --cflags json-c
-I/usr/include/json-c

issue_title: Document keyring usage on Linux
issue_body:
We used to have to use SecretStorage-Setup a package I wrote to link in some system libraries to virtualenvs.

However, with the latest python packages available, it seems like there are just [two packages to be installed on an Ubuntu system](https://github.com/jaraco/keyring#using-keyring-on-ubuntu-1604) (I tested 14.04) to get the keyring to work using the dbus secretstorage subsystem.  

- keyring: Keg already includes this a dependency
- SecretStorage: but keyring now includes this as a dependency on Linux
- dbus-python: can be `pip install`ed

So, for any application that wants to use Keyring substitution on Linux, just `pip install dbus-python`.

- [ ] document this in the readme at least
- [ ] remove keyring help messages that talk about SecretStorage-Setup, adjust messages as needed
- [ ] remove SecreteStorage-Setup repo or, at least, put up notice of deprecation

issue_title: rrule parsing allows `UNTIL` with non-UTC dates
issue_body:
```shell
~ cat utc_until.py && python utc_until.py
```

```python
from datetime import datetime
import dateutil.rrule

rrule = dateutil.rrule.rrulestr(
    'DTSTART;TZID=America/New_York:20180601T120000 RRULE:FREQ=DAILY;INTERVAL=1;UNTIL=20180602T000000'
)
print rrule.after(datetime.utcnow())
```

```python
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "utc_until.py", line 7, in <module>
    print rrule.after(datetime.utcnow())
  File "/home/ryan/.local/share/virtualenvs/tmp.jQo0RUu68U-Zv6c7Bw0/lib/python2.7/site-packages/dateutil/rrule.py", line 223, in after
    for i in gen:
  File "/home/ryan/.local/share/virtualenvs/tmp.jQo0RUu68U-Zv6c7Bw0/lib/python2.7/site-packages/dateutil/rrule.py", line 862, in _iter
    if until and res > until:
TypeError: can't compare offset-naive and offset-aware datetimes
```

According to RFC5545 Section 3.3.10:
https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc5545#section-3.3.10

> If the "DTSTART" property is specified as a date with UTC time or a date with local time and time zone reference, then the UNTIL rule part MUST be specified as a date with UTC time.

issue_title: Avoid transferring Track models
issue_body:
Mopidy v1.0 API has moved to a lightweight Ref model in many places. This reduces the amount of (often unwanted) data transfered in API calls. You can also pass a list of uris to some methods instead of a list of tracks. This is quicker and prevents bugs such as #49.

The old version of these methods and non uris arguments are now deprecated. I see no need to support older versions of Mopidy so we should rework the webclient to make full use of all these improvements.

issue_title: Bug in formatting mathjax/LaTeX inconsistently depending on chosen delimiters, markdown interaction
issue_body:
I just discovered that the notebook formats the same LaTeX input in different ways depending upon your choice of equation delimiter for LaTeX/mathjax which traditionally should not matter.

An example of this bug (plus a png of the correctly rendered incorrect rendering, since github apparently has its own bugs regarding displaying LaTeX/mathjax embedded in notebooks) can be found at https://gist.github.com/michaelpacer/2b627ffe8f10cde53926.

Put briefly, it's not supposed to matter whether you choose to delimit LaTeX formatted math with `\(…\)` and `$…$` for inline equations or `\[…\]` or `$$…$$` for displayed equations. Of course, because `\` is used as an escape character we have to instead use`\\(…\\)` `\\[…\\]`, which in theory is fine. However, because LaTeX formatted subscripts use the `_` symbol, and that conflicts with markdown's interpretation of `_…_` as an indicator of italics, it seems that notebook parses these two different ways of delimiting equations differently. If you use `\\(…\\)` `\\[…\\]` then you need to escape underscores `\_` but that means that your LaTeX is now invalid outside of the notebook (as can be seen in the rendering of `\_` when using `$…$` and `$$…$$` as the delimiters). 

For now it seems safer to guide people to just use `$…$` and `$$…$$`, since then at least it will interpret valid LaTeX properly (this has the unfortunate side-effect of conflicting with one of the [LaTeX "deadly sins"](ftp://ftp.dante.de/tex-archive/info/l2tabu/english/l2tabuen.pdf), but I think that's a less big issue than writing completely invalid LaTeX, imho). 

I'm not sure if there is going to be an easy or even possible fix for this (since I know this kind of parsing is incredibly difficult to manage) but I was befuddled with why my properly formatted LaTeX was not being rendered at all, so I figured I'd bring it to people's attention.

issue_title: Cloudwatch Resource Metrics falsely equivalent
issue_body:
This might possibly be partly caused by the requirement of an identifier to be present in a Resource object (where Dimensions are not required to be present in a Metric or Alarm but Namespace and Name are, and are currently used as identifiers in Metric Resources), but two Metrics with the same namespace and name should not be considered equivalent -- Dimensions should be used as a secondary identifier for uniqueness, since it is additionally required to obtain that specific Metric's statistics. 

Reproduction of bug:

```
>>> h = cloudwatch.metrics.filter(Namespace="AWS/EC2", MetricName='DiskReadBytes')
>>> m = [metric for metric in h]
>>> m
[cloudwatch.Metric(namespace='AWS/EC2', name='DiskReadBytes'), cloudwatch.Metric(namespace='AWS/EC2', name='DiskReadBytes'), cloudwatch.Metric(namespace='AWS/EC2', name='DiskReadBytes'), ...]
>>> m[0] == m[1]
True
>>> m[0].dimensions == m[1].dimensions
False
```

issue_title: No Waiter available for attach_role_policy
issue_body:
Scenario
=======
I call `iam_client.create_role()` and `iam_client.attach_role_policy()` to prepare for a `lambda_client.create_function()` call but receive the following error:
```
botocore.errorfactory.InvalidParameterValueException: An error occurred (InvalidParameterValueException) when calling the CreateFunction operation: The role defined for the function cannot be assumed by Lambda.
```
However, if I wait for 10-15 seconds after creating the role and attaching the policy, the create function call succeeds. 

Interpretation
===========
It obviously takes some time for AWS to process/commit changes to roles and attached policies. Normally, boto would provide a Waiter to handle this scenario but none is available. Perhaps one is required? At the very least, boto documentation should be updated to warn users that waiting is required.

This interpretation is corroborated in https://stackoverflow.com/questions/36419442/the-role-defined-for-the-function-cannot-be-assumed-by-lambda

Details
======

issue_title: ListType mock objects: min values for 'min_size' and 'max_size'
issue_body:
It's not possible to generate empty mock list using `ListType`, because it uses bool-like checks:

```python

min_size = self.min_size or 1
max_size = self.max_size or 1
```

Default value for these attributes is `None`, so it's better to compare values with `None`:

```python

min_size = self.min_size if self.min_size is not None else 1
max_size = self.max_size if self.max_size is not None else 1
```

Thanks

issue_title: flatten / expand
issue_body:
What's the use case for `flatten()` and `expand()`? Do we want to keep them?

`ListType` has a weird feature specifically to support `expand`: it accepts dictionaries and uses their keys to sort the values. This is so that the list

```
field = ['foo', 'bar']
```

can be flattened into

```
{'field.0': 'foo', 'field.1': 'bar'}
```

and successfully expanded without confusing it with a dictionary with keys 0 and 1.

I'm not okay with `ListType` accepting dictionaries. To work around this, I taught `expand` to interpret a flattened structure with a zero-based key sequence as a list. This works, but it means that a legitimate dictionary with (only) zero-based integer keys will also become a list on expansion. Is this good enough? Does anyone use `flatten`?

The alternative is to rewrite `expand()` so that it actually looks at the model it's expanding to, but I don't want to go there if this is just a vestigial feature that no one uses.

issue_title: Wrong custom service class definition when the name is sorted before 'MXNetVisionService'
issue_body:
https://github.com/awslabs/mxnet-model-server/blob/f98a2763f71695a59a69974c367c6f8e43203373/mms/mxnet_model_server.py#L150

The assumption that "# The overrided class is the last one in class_defs" is wrong because 'class_defs' is a list returned by inspect.getmembers(), which returns a list of member classes names sorted by the name. 
https://docs.python.org/2/library/inspect.html#inspect.getmembers

So, using "mode_class_name = class_defs[-1].__name__" leads to wrong class name when the class name string is something smaller than the base class name.

Thanks.

issue_title: Memory leak
issue_body: A few days ago while investigating https://github.com/ni/nimi-python/issues/268, I left the following code running for a few days (by accident, forgot to stop it): ``` import niswitch import time #with niswitch.Session('2532', False, True, '') as session: i = 0 while True: start = time.time() with niswitch.Session('', False, False, 'Simulate=1, DriverSetup=topology:2532/1-Wire 4x128 Matrix') as session: assert session.scan_mode == niswitch.ScanMode.BREAK_BEFORE_MAKE i = i + 1 end = time.time() print("Iteration {0}: {1:06.3f} seconds".format(i, (end-start), " seconds")) ``` A few days later, I'm pleasantly surprised that the program is still going strong after more than 375,000 iterations. But when I looked at Task Manager I was unpleasantly surprised to see that the python3.exe process was using 1,162,000 KB or RAM! Now, the program is creating and destroying a simulated DAQmx-based NI-SWITCH device. It's possible that the memory leak comes from the driver itself. If that's the case, we need to file an NI internal bug report. But it could also be that our Python code is somehow leaking memory in which case we need to fix it. Easiest way is to repeat the test without using simulation and/or using simulation on a CA4 device. If there's no leak then we can rule out Python bindings.

issue_title: Weird float behavior around conversions?
issue_body:
I've the following benchmark:

```
(set-option :produce-models true)
(set-logic QF_FP)

; A free variable x
(declare-fun x () (_ FloatingPoint 11 53))

; Let y be the ulp
(define-fun  y () (_ FloatingPoint 11 53) (fp #b0 #b00000000000 #x0000000000001))

; Assert that x and y are floating-point equal.
(assert (fp.eq x y))

; Compute the conversion from double-to-float for each
(define-fun xf () (_ FloatingPoint 8 24) ((_ to_fp 8 24) roundNearestTiesToEven x))
(define-fun yf () (_ FloatingPoint 8 24) ((_ to_fp 8 24) roundNearestTiesToEven y))

; Results should be precisely the same float; so the following should be unsat
(assert (not (= xf yf)))

(check-sat)

(get-model)
```

It looks hairy; but it's essentially creating the smallest double value in `y`, and then asserts that `x` is floating-point equal to `y`. Which would only be satisfiable when `x` is also the smallest double. Then it goes ahead and casts them both to single-precision values; and asserts that the result should be the different. That query should be unsatisfiable; as both conversions should yield `+0`.

But when run, Z3 produces:

```
sat
(model
  (define-fun x () (_ FloatingPoint 11 53)
    (fp #b0 #b00000000000 #x0000000000001))
)
```

What's weird is that it says the model is satisfiable; but then suggests `x` must be the smallest double itself; i.e., in the model it created, the conversion to single-precision through `xf` and `yf` should be patently equal; thus making the query itself `unsat`.

issue_title: ModelChain with weather data
issue_body:
Some time ago we started the [feedinlib](https://github.com/oemof/feedinlib) project to calculated the output of wind and pv power plants with a given weather data set. The pv part of the feedinlib is an application of the pvlib similar to the ModelChain, which did not exist when we started.

In my opinion a ModelChain with weather data is better located within the pvlib, so I would like to move this functionality from the feedinlib to the pvlib. The structure should be similar to the existing ModelChain while the content could be base on the existing [feedinlib model](https://github.com/oemof/feedinlib/blob/dev/feedinlib/models.py#L56).

My idea is to define a weather class containing the weather data and the meta data of the weather data set similar to the [weather class of the feedinlib](https://github.com/oemof/feedinlib/blob/dev/feedinlib/weather.py#L11). A weather object could be passed to a new ModelChain or to an expanded ModelChain.

I would like to discuss the idea before creating a PR to avoid obsolete work.

What do you think of this idea?

issue_title: advice on modeling at high altitudes?
issue_body:
I am interested in modeling clear sky irradiance at different altitudes, including the stratosphere. My test run using my current clear sky setup with ineichen at an altitude of 20km produced an answer significantly greater than the dni_et I used. 

I'd appreciate any hints as to whether I need use something other than the default linke turbidities table, or whether there is a better algorithm for clear sky to use for high altitude modeling.

issue_title: Inverter model supporting temperature derate?
issue_body:
The snlinverter doesn't support an adjustment for temperature derating. My experiments with implementing it after the fact show a gap due to the way the coefficients define the curve to the maximum output, Pac0, which changes based on temperature. 

I saw that in issue #2 there was a request for a new inverter model. Does that model support temperature derate, or should I work on defining an snlinverter_with_temperature_derate? I think it should be a separate function because the snl inverter model definition doesn't seem to include this concept, and we probably want to keep our models consistent with their references?

issue_title: Passing nullptr to function
issue_body:
I understand that in the documentation, `None` is translated into `nullptr`.
I have a function like this:
```c++
void dataStart(void (*dataNotifyIncrease)(void *ptr), void (*dataNotifyDecrease)(void *ptr),
		void *dataNotifyUserPtr, void (*dataShutdownNotify)(void *ptr), void *dataShutdownUserPtr) const {
		bool success = caerDeviceDataStart(handle.get(), dataNotifyIncrease, dataNotifyDecrease, dataNotifyUserPtr,
			dataShutdownNotify, dataShutdownUserPtr);
		if (!success) {
			throw std::runtime_error("Failed to start getting data.");
		}
	}
```

And I bind the function by
```c++
    py::class_<device> ddevice(pydevices, "device");
    ddevice
        .def("dataStart", &device::dataStart);
```

However when I call the function in python

```python
dvs128_handle.dataStart(None, None, None, None, None)
```

It reports the error

```
 dvs128_handle.dataStart(None, None, None, None, None)
TypeError: dataStart(): incompatible function arguments. The following argument types are supported:
    1. (self: pycaer.devices.device, arg0: void (void*), arg1: void (void*), arg2: capsule, arg3: void (void*), arg4: capsule) -> None

Invoked with: <pycaer.devices.dvs128 object at 0x1082dc9d0>, None, None, None, None, None
```

Apparently, the original C++ example is like this

```c++
dvs128Handle.dataStart(nullptr, nullptr, nullptr, nullptr, nullptr);
```

Any solution I can pass `nullptr` as `None` or other solution for `nullptr` in pybind11?

issue_title: Notice: Breaking changes with CLI commands
issue_body:
Due to the migration to Click for the CLI in 1.0.0 of blobxfer, the existing argument and parameter structure will change from the 0.x.y releases.

Note that the docker image tagged as latest will not update to 1.0.0 until it reaches release candidate status. PyPI packages in the 1.x release line prior to rc will be hidden and will only be able to be installed with the `--pre` flag or targeting a specific version. You can always version your pip install, e.g., `pip install blobxfer==x.y.z`, which will tie your use case with the version `x.y.z` of blobxfer.

Thanks for your understanding as we improve the overall blobxfer experience and feature set.

[VSTS] Release/build creation assumes that Git 2.7+ is installed
issue_body: When trying to run `az vsts build create` or `az vsts release create` on a Git-managed project, I get the following error: I'm running OS X El Capitan, and have Git 2.6.4 installed. The `git remote get-url` method wasn't added until [v2.7.0](https://git-scm.com/docs/git-remote/2.7.0), which explains why the above error occurs. When I updated my Git installation, the error was resolved, but it would be ideal if the commands either let you know that you need a newer version of Git to continue, or employed a workaround of some kind.

issue_title: [Question] Expected flow for identifying resource types when using table-based output
issue_body:
**Note: As I was writing out this bug, I realized the mentioned workaround, but I've left the original text in place in order to articulate my experience in case that is valuable.**

I was trying to run the `az resource show` command on a function app instance, and I wasn't able to figure out how to correctly determine the value to provide to the required `--resource-type` flag. Running `az resource list` easily provided the name and resource group values, but it's somewhat confusing that it displays a "Kind" value, which doesn't seem to correlate to the `--resource-type` flag? I tried appending "Microsoft.Web/" to the "kind" that was displayed via the `az resource list` command (i.e. "functionapp"), but that also didn't work. In the end, I went into the Azure portal, and viewed the "underlying" ARM template in order to determine that the type is actually "Microsoft.Web/sites". 

Once I was using that value, `az resource show` worked as expected, however, I couldn't help but feel like I'm clearly missing something. Is there a command to determine the list of appropriate type names? I found that `az resource provider list` showed me the list of providers (called "namespaces" in the help text for the `--resource-type` flag?), but I wasn't able to determine the list of available types/kinds within that provider via `az resource show`.

In the end, I realized that I was probably only getting a subset of the available metadata from the `az resource list` command, due to having configured the CLI to default to table-based output, so I  ran `az resource list --output json` and saw the correct resource type value.

Maybe I'm the only person that would get confused by this, but as a topic of conversation, I wonder whether it wouldn't be valuable to consider one or more of the following:

1. Display an example of what the resource type value should be in the command help text, to provide some kind of indication as to what it is. If someone wasn't familiar with ARM concepts, would they know what *<namespace>/<type>* is? Maybe, I'm not sure.

2. Display the resource type in the table-based output of the `az resource list` command. It seems like this value is important, since it's a required param to the detail counterpart of the `resource list` command, so maybe it's worth promoting to the table. Maybe not.

3. Provide some kind of indication somewhere as to how to acquire the resource type value. Doing #2 would probably be sufficient, but in the absence of that, maybe it would be worth having some kind of help text in the `az resource show` (and others?) command to point users in the right direction.

### Environment summary

**Install Method:** `curl -L https://aka.ms/InstallAzureCli | bash`

vm (2.0.2)

Python (Linux) 2.7.13 (default, Jan 13 2017, 10:15:16) 
[GCC 6.3.1 20161221 (Red Hat 6.3.1-1)]
```

**OS Version:** Linux Fedora 24 (4.9.13-101.fc24.x86_64)

**Shell Type:** bash

### Description

When running a zone import e.g.:

```
az network dns zone import -g my-rg -n mydomain.com -f mydomain-zonefile.txt
```

where `mydomain-zonefile.txt` has the following content:

```
@     600     IN      SOA     ignored.com. hostmaster.mydomain.com. (
                                2017040400
                                28800
                                7200
                                604800
                                600
                                )

test    3600    IN      A       2.2.2.2
```

The relative domain record "test" is imported into Azure DNS as absolute value `test.mydomain.com` instead of relative value `test`, meaning that lookup `test.mydomain.com.mydomain.com` works and `test.mydomain.com` fails.

At the moment this isn't possible and returns with the following error: ``` azure.common.AzureHttpError: The metadata specified is invalid. It has characters that are not permitted. InvalidMetadataThe metadata specified is invalid. It has characters that are not permitted. RequestId:a91a1861-0001-0067-3cf0-7b83d1000000 Time:2016-03-11T23:47:14.5882290Z ``` This EXACT scenario isn't required/necessary, but it is the crux of the problem where I'd simply like to 'append' metadata to existing metadata. In order to work around this I suspect (unless someone points out otherwise) I will need to strip out the x-ms-meta- prefix out of the keys and THEN I can resubmit. That's a lot of extra cycles for the developer, as well as the computer that do nothing but get in the way.

issue_title: Return Virtual Machine Instance View from list() or list_all() methods
issue_body:
Is there a way to get an instance view returned from the list() or list_all() methods of the VirtualMachineOperations class?

Building the Azure inventory script for Ansible. Fetching the list of machines for a subscription of for a specific resource group returns data within 3 or 4 seconds. Is there a more efficient way to do this?

issue_title: Handle eggs for libraries with versions containing _ in the filename
issue_body: In 1.1.3, there was a change that caused the versions of packages to not be normalized anymore. This is a problem because the egg filename spec normalizes versions using to_filename, which turns '-'s into '_'s. So, any egg with a version containing an '-' or any character normalized to a '-' can't be resolved with pex's resolver.

issue_title: Python 3 - Thrift resolution failing for codegen
issue_body:
## Issue
`test_apache_thrift_py_gen.py`'s `test_namespace_effective()` fails with 
```                    
 E       pex.resolver.Untranslateable: Package SourcePackage('https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/3c/69/50ee9e3ed9663f97eb506a15c1dfce6224b5ac98674ddc008b51b9d0ae3b/thrift-0.9.2.tar.gz#sha256=08f665e4b033c9d2d0b6174d869273104362c80e77ee4c01054a74141e378afa') is not translateable by ChainedTranslator(WheelTranslator, EggTranslator, SourceTranslator)
```

## Reproduce
1. `./pants clean-all`
1. `./pants --python-setup-interpreter-constraints='["CPython>=3.5"]' test tests/python/pants_test/backend/codegen/thrift/python:`

## Hacky solution
```
diff --git a/tests/python/pants_test/backend/codegen/thrift/python/test_apache_thrift_py_gen.py b/tests/python/pants_test/backend/codegen/thrift/python/test_apache_thrift_py_gen.py
index 789db8834..8b14f751f 100644
--- a/tests/python/pants_test/backend/codegen/thrift/python/test_apache_thrift_py_gen.py
+++ b/tests/python/pants_test/backend/codegen/thrift/python/test_apache_thrift_py_gen.py
@@ -157,7 +157,6 @@ class ApacheThriftPyGenTest(TaskTestBase):
     pythonpath = list(interpreter.extras.values())
     pythonpath.extend(os.path.join(get_buildroot(), t.target_base) for t in targets)
     for dist in resolve(['thrift=={}'.format(self.get_thrift_version(apache_thrift_gen))],
-                        interpreter=interpreter,
                         context=python_repos.get_network_context(),
                         fetchers=python_repos.get_fetchers()):
       pythonpath.append(dist.location)
```

I don't think this is the solution we want though. We must be choosing an interpreter from the interpreter_cache in the above lines for a reason. 


### thrift/java
`tests/python/pants_test/backend/codegen/thrift/java:java` works already, at least locally. If someone ends up submitting a PR to fix `tests/python/pants_test/backend/codegen/thrift/python:python`, also please remove java from `known_py3_failures.txt`.

* **aiohttp-devtools version:**
0.6.4

2.3.8

1.0
* **python version:**
3.6.1
* **Platform:**
Linux Ubuntu 16.04
### Issue Summary
aiohttp-devtools don't work with yarl library version 1.0.
Reason - https://github.com/aio-libs/aiohttp-devtools/blob/33ef2dc75a30264fe26bdaf69ea3c6d0fbc9a62b/aiohttp_devtools/runserver/serve.py#L15 (at least). I don't check all files, maybe there are any other places with similar error.

issue_title: Runserver loop parameter
issue_body:
Hi.

If I use uvloop for my project as
```python
asyncio.set_event_loop_policy(uvloop.EventLoopPolicy())
```
and run `runserver` command and press `ctrl+c`, then I get an error
```python
RuntimeError: Task <Task pending coro=<AppTask._run() running at /../lib/python3.6/site-packages/aiohttp_devtools/runserver/watch.py:48>> got Future <Future pending cb=[_chain_future.<locals>._call_check_cancel()]> attached to a different loop
```
Also, the server does not restart if I change the code.

Maybe, `runserver` command should take `--loop` parameter (for example `--loop=uvloop`) ?
Or already there is a solution to this problem?

Thanks.

issue_title: NTA_CONF_PATH assumes colon splits directory paths
issue_body: Change to use os.pathsep instead of a colon. Windows uses a semi-colon, for example.

issue_title: Reserved slots to unknown categories in CategoryEncoder
issue_body:
Sometimes you have access to a dataset for training, but are not sure if there will be any new categories in the future. The Adaptative Scalar Encoder is not an ideal option, because it means that the SP and TM will have to re-map the adapted bits, loosing some of the work that has been done. The Random Scalar Encoder does not work well in certain situations, when you need to ensure each category is treated completely separately. I came up with a small update to the Category Encoder. When you create it, you can reserve some slots for potential new categories. When a new category is fed, the encoder maps it to one of the previously reserved slots without affecting any of the other categories. If the encoder runs out of slots, newer categories will be treated as Unknown.

I already made a pull request for this, sorry for not opening the issue before ( #3093 ).

issue_title: Should LAMMPS in Atomate be deprecated
issue_body:
Lammps in atomate was a project @matk86 and I started. At the time my research project seemed like it necessitated that, but in the end I went a different direction and the code was never really finished. I don't think either @matk86 or I am interested in maintaining/updating Lammps in atomate (at least in the next 6-8 months). So it seems like we should deprecate it unless someone wants to take over. 

I recently heard from @adengz that he is going to overhaul the lammps io in pymatgen (which can certainly be improved), but the most recent commits break the lammps atomate tests. So a little motivation to upgrade/fix or deprecate.

Thoughts???
@shyuep @computron 

Please submit help issues to:
https://groups.google.com/forum/#!forum/atomate

The Github issues is no longer used except for internal development purposes.

If you are unable to use the Google Group, you may submit an issue here, but you must **clearly** state the reason you are unable to use the Google Group in your ticket. Otherwise, your issue will be **closed** without response.

issue_title: datalab/bigquery/_utils.py parse_table_name should support Standard SQL table naming

      name: the name of the table either as a string or a 3-part tuple (projectid, datasetid, name).

issue_title: schema `validate` only uses `load_from` name
issue_body:
In our code every field is aliased using `load_from`. (This is a simplified example). 
```python
class ExampleSchema(Schema):

    session_id = fields.String(load_from='si', validate=[Length(equal=12)])
    geo_location = fields.String(load_from='gl', required=True)  # input looks like '80,80'

    @validates('geo_location')
    def validate_geo_location(self, value):
        latitude, longitude = value.split(',')
        latitude = int(latitude)
        if latitude < -90 or latitude > 90:
            raise ValidationError('Latitude is out of range'))
```

Currently, ExampleSchema.validates returns errors like this:
`{'si': [u'Length must be 12.'], 'gl': [u'Missing data for required field.']}`

Notice that the field name in the validation errors refers to the `load_from` name and not the name supplied by ExampleSchema().fields, which makes sense, but we would like to have something like `validate_to` to ensure that this output looks like this:
`{'session_id': [u'Length must be 12.'], 'geo_location': [u'Missing data for required field.']}`

A possibly important note, if we have a validation error for the custom validator it *DOES* gives us our desired behavior, but only for that field that has been custom validated, i.e.:

`{'si': [u'Length must be 12.'], 'geo_location': ['Latitude is out of range']}`

I tried playing around with `dump_to`, `attribute`, etc. but they only seem to change the field name in successfully serialized data, not in errors.

Thanks for any help/updates! I can add more clarity if necessary.

issue_title: Email address validation fails when mailbox name contains non-ASCII characters
issue_body:
Hi there,

It appears email address validation fails when non-ASCII characters are present in the mailbox name, which should be permitted as per [RFC 6530](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Email_address#Internationalization). See example (tested on ```dev``` branch):

```
In [1]: from marshmallow import fields, Schema

In [2]: class Test(Schema):
   ...:     email = fields.Email()
   ...:

In [3]: t = Test()

In [4]: t.load({'email': u'josé@blah.com'})
Out[4]: UnmarshalResult(data={}, errors={'email': [u'"jos\xe9@blah.com" is not a valid email address.']})
```

Chars outside of ASCII seem to work fine in the domain though.

Thanks,

Lex

issue_title: fields.Field's missing option with many=True, unexpected behavior
issue_body:
marshmallow's fields.Field has two options.

- default
- missing

I wonder `default` is the hook (or default value) for serialization, and `missing` is the hook for deserialization.

## with many=True option, default is ok

when serialization, default option is correctly, I feel.

```python
from marshmallow import fields, Schema


class S(Schema):
    nums = fields.Integer(many=True, default=[1, 2, 3])

print(S().dump({}).data)
# {'nums': [1, 2, 3]}
```

## with many=true option,  missing is uncomfortable behavior

But,  when deserialization,  current marshmallow's behavior is not good. Maybe, I have misunderstanding for missing option.  Although, using missing option with `many=True`,  treating default value as collection is better, I think.

```python
from marshmallow import fields, Schema


class S(Schema):
    nums = fields.Integer(many=True, missing="[1, 2, 3]")

# this is error.
print(S().load({}))
# UnmarshalResult(data={}, errors={'nums': ['Not a valid integer.']})


# if missing's value is 1, error is not raised. but, this is not good, at least me.
class S2(Schema):
    nums = fields.Integer(many=True, missing="1")
print(S2().load({}))
# UnmarshalResult(data={'nums': 1}, errors={})
```

issue_title: proposal: infinitely-recursive model schemas
issue_body:
Right now if I generate a schema for a model which has related objs, those will be represented by `Related` fields, which are just pks. This is obviously the correct default behavior, but for my purposes it would be useful if I could explicitly ask to generate an infinitely-recursive (and thus unserializable) schema, perhaps like

```
class MySchema(ModelSchema):
    class Meta:
        model = MyModel
        infinitely_recursive = True
```

If you look at my [tests for marshmallow-select](https://github.com/marshmallow-code/marshmallow-select/blob/master/tests/test_marshmallow_select.py) (see fixtures `schemas` & `detail_schemas`) hopefully you get the idea. I'd like to generate an infinitely-recursive "Master Schema" for every model I want to serialize, then define several finite subtrees of that schema for each view of the model's data I present in my application.

At the moment, the only way to do this (that I know of) is to define parent schemas with ModelSchema and then manually override each relation field with marshmallow's two-way nesting. Which is fine for the present example (and for fixing the underlying issues), but is going to get boring as I try to scale this up to larger applications.

Note that the aforementioned test is currently broken, and I probably won't have time to fix it until at least this weekend, so the need here is not urgent, but it would be seriously cool if this could be implemented somehow eventually. My knowledge of the marshmallow-sqlalchemy internals is not very deep, but I assume it's at least possible. Is it hard?

I know I'm not the first person to ask something _like_ this, so there's probably lots of other marginally related issues, but I'm not sure whether to link them.

issue_title: Table.__getitem__ causing PyCharm debugger to enter a recursive loop
issue_body:
I'm running web2py 2.11.2 & pydal 15.02.27 on python 2.7.11 using Pycharm 2017.3.3.
It seems that the Pycharm debugger stops working and just executes without stopping as if it was in normal run mode whenever stepping over a line that calls `Table.with_alias`.
Although no errors are thrown, It does prevent me from running the debugger after this line.
After further digging, the problem seems to be in `Table.__getitem__`.
`BaseAdapter.alias` is executing `copy.copy(table)` which in turn creates a new Table object using `Table.__new__(Table)` . The created object isn't initialized & vars returns an empty dictionary.
When `__getitem__` is called from `copy._reconstruct` `_db` isn't defined, so when the line 
`isgoogle = key != '_db' and 'google' in self._db._drivers_available and \`
is evaluated the PyCharm debugger enters a recursive loop in `__getitem__` with the key `'_db'`.
Here's a fix I did on my copy to overcome this:
`isgoogle = key != '_db' and 'google' in self._db._drivers_available and \`

I'm not sure if this happens on other IDEs, but this bug can be easily fixed.
Thanks.

issue_title: Providing a unicode string to dmatrices results in a misleading error message
issue_body:
Doing this works fine:
foo = pd.DataFrame(dict(a=[1,2.0], b=[4,5.0]))
patsy.dmatrices('a ~ b', foo)

However, if the formula is given by a unicode string:
patsy.dmatrices(u'a ~ b', foo)

An exception is raised:
ValueError: design matrix must be real-valued floating point

The exception is very misleading about the nature of the problem.  I thought an inf or nan had crept into the dataframe, but no bad values were there.

One possible fix is to send the first argument of dmatrices through the str() function:
patsy.dmatrices(str(u'a ~ b'), foo)

Another possible fix is to raise print a different error message so that the user has some clear idea of the cause of the problem. 

I'm using patsy version 0.3.0

Thanks!

issue_title: jobnum Segment Shows '0' Regardless of show_zero Setting When Using zpython
issue_body:
### Issue
If you are using `zpython` in `zsh`, the `jobnum` segment will show `0` when there are zero jobs regardless of the `show_zero` setting.

### Expected Behavior
If there are zero background jobs, and `show_zero` is false, instead of displaying `0`, `powerline` should hide the segment.

### Recreating
This issue can be recreated by including the `jobnum` segment in your shell config and using `zsh` with `zpython` as the `powerline` renderer. I believe that `jobnum` is included in the default shell config, and by default `show_zero` is false.

### Cause
`jobnum()` in `powerline/segments/shell.py` treats `segment_info['args'].jobnum` as type `int` (and that is correct when `zpython` isn't being used). However, the `Args.jobnum` property in `powerline/bindings/zsh/__init__.py ` has a type of `str`. Since `'0' != 0`, the segment is shown.

### Solution
Update the `Args.jobnum` property to convert the type to `int`.

As I identified the cause and solution, I will make the changes and submit a pull request.

issue_title: High CPU usage caused by the Hazelcast client
issue_body:
Ever since we added the HZ client to one of our components, CPU usage of that component increased significantly. We could track it down to the `select` syscall, which is used by [`asyncore.loop` called by AsyncoreReactor](https://github.com/hazelcast/hazelcast-python-client/blob/master/hazelcast/reactor.py#L37).

CPU usage could be reduced by changing that call to `loop(count=1, timeout=0.1)`.

Our application itself makes heavy use of asyncore, so running two threads in one process, each with its own asyncore main loop in it, doesn't really make much sense. We'd appreciate being able to have the HZ client use our main loop, instead of living on its own thread. Would that be feasible?

issue_title: PolymorphicModel fails if swapped with another Model in Django 1.10
issue_body:
I developed a reusable app called `alpha` that provides the `Alpha` model (that is expected to get inherited by another model `AnotherAlpha`) using `django-polymorphic` and `swapper`.  Then created the `beta` app that inherits `alpha`'s `AlphaAbstract` so that users can decide to use a different Model instead of the default.  After swapping to `beta`'s implementation of `Alpha` i get this error:

```
  [...]
  File "/home/ghelo/PycharmProjects/capricorn/PolySwapper/alpha/models.py", line 10, in <module>
    class Alpha(AlphaAbstract):
  File "/home/ghelo/PycharmProjects/capricorn/lib/python3.6/site-packages/polymorphic/base.py", line 99, in __new__
    self.validate_model_manager(new_class.objects, model_name, 'objects')
  File "/home/ghelo/PycharmProjects/capricorn/lib/python3.6/site-packages/django/db/models/manager.py", line 198, in __get__
    cls._meta.swapped,

AttributeError: Manager isn't available; 'alpha.Alpha' has been swapped for 'beta.Beta'
```

---------------------
## Replication

**1.** Run these commands:
```bash
pip install Django
django-admin startproject PolySwapper
cd PolySwapper
django-admin startapp alpha
django-admin startapp beta
pip install django-polymorphic swapper
```
As of reporting this issue the package versions are:
```
Django==1.10.6
swapper==1.0.0
django-polymorphic==1.1
```
**2.** Create models Alpha and Beta
```python
# alpha/models.py
import swapper
from polymorphic.models import PolymorphicModel
class AlphaAbstract(PolymorphicModel):
    class Meta:
        abstract = True
class Alpha(AlphaAbstract):
    class Meta:
        swappable = swapper.swappable_setting('alpha', "Alpha")
class AnotherAlpha(Alpha):
    pass

# beta/models.py
from alpha.models import AlphaAbstract
class Beta(AlphaAbstract):
    pass
```
**2.** Run `./manage.py makemigrations`
**3.** Add `alpha` and `beta` to `settings.py`:
```python
INSTALLED_APPS = [
    ...
    'alpha',
    'beta',
]
```
**4.** Append the following to `settings.py` like so:
```python
ALPHA_ALPHA_MODEL = "beta.Beta"
```

**5.** Run `./manage.py shell`  (or any command)
And you'll get the error:
```
AttributeError: Manager isn't available; 'alpha.Alpha' has been swapped for 'beta.Beta'
```

## Note on Django 1.9

I tried replicating this issue using `Django~1.9` and i get a different error, but i think this is a bug with Django:
```
alpha.AnotherAlpha.alpha_ptr: (fields.E301) Field defines a relation with the model 'alpha.Alpha', which has been swapped out.
	HINT: Update the relation to point at 'settings.ALPHA_ALPHA_MODEL'.
```

This can be fixed by explicitly declaring the parent_link, like so:
```python
class AnotherAlpha(Alpha):
    alpha_ptr = models.OneToOneField(
        auto_created=True, on_delete=CASCADE,
        parent_link=True, primary_key=True, serialize=False,
        to=swapper.get_model_name('alpha', "Alpha"))
```

After that the issue never gets raised.

issue_title: Display Warning for outdated parameters
issue_body:
Hi there!

I really enjoy OpenFisca, but I recently encountered an issue.

### Here is what I did:
I ran a simulation on the year 2017, with outdated parameters (more specifically the piecewise linear tax scheme in OpenFisca-France).

### Here is what I expected to happen:
I expected to have results for the 2017 income tax.

### Here is what actually happened:
The last inputted parameters in OF-France were those of 2015, I thus had results for the 2015 income tax instead of the 2017 one ((the problem is solved)[https://github.com/openfisca/openfisca-france/issues/885#issuecomment-375302048]).

###  Here is what I would expect to happen:

If I run a simulation on outdated parameters, I would expect to get a `Warning` stating that the parameters may be outdated.

### That can help you reproduce this issue:
Launch any simulation for the year 2042.

### Here is a possible solution to that issue.

Parameters are inputted in yaml files, if a parameter is inputted or actualised by a contributor, she could add a potential expiration date.

Let's say for a parameter that is expected to be actualized every year:

```
brackets:
- rate:
    2017-01-01:
      value: 0.3
  threshold:
    2017-01-01:
      value: 27086.0
- rate:
    2018-01-01:
      value: unactualized
  threshold:
    2018-01-01:
      value: unactualized
```

Then if a simulation is ran for a date greater or equal to 2018, a `Warning` stating that xx number of  parameters are possibly outdated would be displayed. 

If the contributor think that a parameter is unlikely to be actualised in the near future, then it would not be necessary to give a potential expiration date ( e.g. the "nombre de parts fiscales" )
  

## Context

I identify more as a:

- Economist.
- Analyst _(I make macroscopic computations on real populations)_.

issue_title: Track API requests with Piwik
issue_body:
On each API call, send a “pageview” to a Piwik instance. This will allow for usage analytics.
This tracking must be nonblocking and have only a minimal impact on performance. A library such as [`grequests`](https://github.com/kennethreitz/grequests) should be used on [Piwik HTTP API](http://developer.piwik.org/api-reference/tracking-api), or a specific wrapper such as [`piwikapi`](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/piwikapi).

The API must still work if the tracker is not installed.

- [x] Create a `openfisca/tracker` package.
- [x] Send a “pageview” on each API call, with the URL being the called endpoint URL.
- [x] Measure performance impact: benchmark the time to handle 100 computations, 100 parameters requests, before and after the change, in 3 different runs.
- [x] Document that behaviour, explain why this is done, explain why there are no CNIL & EU privacy requirements in the README of that package.
- [x] Add a target URL setup to opt-in to tracking (`TRACKER=https://stats.openfisca.org`).
- [x] Add an information line in the doc.
- [x] Output a reassuring information message on API start if tracking is set up.

issue_title: Curator rollover documentation does not match Elasticsearch rollover API documentation
issue_body:
### Expected Behavior
Curator should have similar controls and behavior as Elasticsearch API

### Actual Behavior
*curator*
When choosing conditions, either one of max_age or max_docs, or both may be used. If both are used, then both conditions must be matched for the rollover to occur.
https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/client/curator/current/rollover.html

*elasticsearch*
Elasticsearch also supports `max_size`, and will rollover if *ANY* condition of the three possible conditions is met.

https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/reference/current/indices-rollover-index.html#CO150-2

CODE_DIR=$(cd $(dirname $0)/.. && pwd)/elasticsearch

issue_title: AngrTranslationError and PyVEXError when Lifter.lift(..., max_size = 0, ...)
issue_body:
The issue is at Line 46 of lifter.py
`max_size = VEX_IRSB_MAX_SIZE if max_size is None else max_size`

If max_size is 0 at the beginning, it would remain 0 all the way through Line 100:
`irsb = pyvex.IRSB(buff, addr, arch,
                                  num_bytes=max_size,
                                  bytes_offset=byte_offset,
                                  traceflags=traceflags)`
PyVex/libvex does not allow a zero max_size and raises an PyVEXError at Line 50 of pyvex/pyvex/block.py

I guess you guys mean "not max_size" rather than "max_size is None". "not max_size" also returns True when max_size is 0. I changed Line 46 and it seems to work
`max_size = VEX_IRSB_MAX_SIZE if not max_size else max_size`

This is just my guess. I'd like to confirm with you guys about it. Let me know if you need the binary to reproduce the error.

issue_body:
Unfortunately the following commit https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio/commit/d4464450e81a6192360fdc0d24bfcda3da7ce145 introduced  a fix, but also a problem.

While working with ``less`` in the last days, I saw that when a bundle needed to be rebuild (because of a file was updated) after a page refresh the following error was being thrown:

```python

File "/home/jmartinm/.virtualenvs/pu-inspire2/src/webassets/src/webassets/env.py", line 731, in __getitem__
key = key.lower()
AttributeError: 'tuple' object has no attribute 'lower'
```

And the problem appears here https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio/commit/d4464450e81a6192360fdc0d24bfcda3da7ce145#diff-dc717d85d0b109c334416b4b5a305f8cR138 as 

```python
ipdb> bundle.filters[0]._options
{'extra_args': (None, 'LESS_EXTRA_ARGS', None), 'line_numbers': (None, 'LESS_LINE_NUMBERS', None), 'less': ('binary', 'LESS_BIN', None), 'run_in_debug': (None, 'LESS_RUN_IN_DEBUG', None), 'paths': (None, 'LESS_PATHS', None)}
ipdb> bundle_copy.filters[0]._options
{'extra_args': (None, (None, 'LESS_EXTRA_ARGS', None), None), 'line_numbers': (None, (None, 'LESS_LINE_NUMBERS', None), None), 'paths': (None, (None, 'LESS_PATHS', None), None), 'run_in_debug': (None, (None, 'LESS_RUN_IN_DEBUG', None), None), 'less': (None, ('binary', 'LESS_BIN', None), None)}
```

The ``deepcopy`` alters the ``_options`` attribute that is later accessed and provokes an error.

I checked that reverting the commit makes this error go away.

issue_title: Improve 404 propagation to Sentry
issue_body:
Currently any 404 raised by at least the `legacy` extension, are propagated to Sentry (when enabled). This is suboptimal, as any wrong URL typed by any user or bot is causing an exception to be recorded.

It would be beneficial to just store those 404 caused by a request having a referer from the current site. At least this would be useful to spot broken links and in which pages they are contained.

issue_title: Add test for concurrent modifications of files
Review all IOStore stuff
issue_body:
As far as I can tell, the inputstore (command line parameter given to all tools) serves no real use.  If this is indeed the case, it should be gotten rid of. 

The outputstore is currently being used to pass data between pipeline phases (index -> map -> call).  This makes sense, but may need to be made optional for performance.  Should think of abstracting this code so it can be toggled in one place.  

The tools also have an out_dir parameter, which seems redundant given that they write to the output store. 

Summary:
* Get rid of input stores
* Get rid of output dir
* Use output store only for writing final output.  But have option to checkpoint intermediate data in output store as done currently.

issue_title: trailing-whitespace-fixer vs. Markdown
issue_body:
The trailing-whitespace hook strips all trailing whitespace, and will match files with a `.md` extension commonly used for Markdown. While GitHub flavored Markdown (and other common variants) will ignore trailing whitespace, the original Markdown specification at [Daring Fireball](http://daringfireball.net/projects/markdown/syntax#p) says: > When you *do* want to insert a `
` break tag using Markdown, you **end a line with two or more spaces**, then type return. *emphasis mine* You could argue that this is a design/spec failure on the level of Make requiring TAB characters and not spaces for rule commands, but the reality is that there are Markdown files that use this feature, and at the very least there ought to be a flag for the trailing-whitespace-fixer that controls whether you want fix Markdown files (in addition to `.md` it is common to see `.markdown` extensions) by normalizing multiple spaces at the end of a non-blank line to 2 spaces or to remove them entirely (a single space at the end of a non-blank line, or any number of spaces on a line that contains only spaces, should always be removed). I would suggest (and might even implement as a PR) something like `--markdown-linebreak-ext=md,markdown` as the default, indicating that files with `.md` or `.markdown` extensions would have multiple spaces at the end of a line normalized to two trailing spaces. If you wanted the trailing spaces stripped in all cases, you would use `--markdown-linebreak-ext` or `--markdown-linebreak-ext=` (or maybe `--no-markdown-linebreak-ext`), if you wanted special Markdown handling for other extensions or all files you would use `--markdown-linebreak-ext=md,txt` or `--markdown-linebreak-ext=*`.

issue_title: pip==9.0.1 fails to correctly install pipenv==11.1.6, but works with pipenv==11.1.5
issue_body:

##### Describe your environment

1. OS Type

Official Python 3.6 Docker image from Docker Hub:

issue_title: Include package version in Pipenv
issue_body:
The version of a package that is installed by pipenv ought to be recorded in the Pipfile. Instead, pipenv records the version as "*" in the Pipfile.

What is the reason for this?

issue_title: Checking if installed packages are up to date is slow and use lots of CPU
issue_body:
I've been trying to migrate a project to use Pipenv but I'm slightly blocked on how much longer it takes for Pipenv to check if the installed dependencies are up to date, compared to pointing `pip install` at a requirements file.

In our setup we run tests inside Docker containers. The image we run the tests on is one that comes pre-installed with the dependencies our project has at the time the image is built. Before we run any tests we then make sure the dependencies are up to date, in case any new dependencies are needed for any new code/tests that might have been added. For this we have just been using `pip install -r requirements.txt`, which normally completes in around 30 seconds when there's no new dependencies to install.

I then tried to switch this to Pipenv and pre-installed the dependences in the image using a `Pipfile` and `Pipfile.lock` and then running `pipenv install --deploy --dev --system` against the files. That works fine and I got an image created, but the problem comes to when we want to run tests and want to check if dependencies are up to date first. I've done this using the same `pipenv install --deploy --dev --system` command and instead of 30 seconds it now takes 5 minutes and 30 seconds! On top of that the CPU usage it much, much higher.

I've made a small test with the `Pipfile` and `Pipfile.lock` we are using (only slightly modified): https://github.com/Tenzer/pipenv-test. Some simple tests that can be run with it is for instance to first install the dependencies and then afterwards check that they are up to date in the local environment, than then see how long time and CPU the second operation takes:

```
$ docker run -it --rm -v $(pwd):/test python bash
root@9f6ecaf12cf8:/# cd /test
root@9f6ecaf12cf8:/test# pip install pipenv
[...]
root@9f6ecaf12cf8:/test# pipenv install --deploy --dev --system
Installing dependencies from Pipfile.lock (f4e26d)…
Ignoring appnope: markers 'sys_platform == "darwin"' don't match your environment
  🐍   ▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉ 212/212 — 00:02:25
root@9f6ecaf12cf8:/test# time pipenv install --deploy --dev --system
Installing dependencies from Pipfile.lock (f4e26d)…
Ignoring appnope: markers 'sys_platform == "darwin"' don't match your environment
  🐍   ▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉ 212/212 — 00:01:04

real	1m7.166s
user	1m49.520s
sys	0m15.000s
```

Note that this was run on my laptop rather than our CI system, and with a slightly simpler `Pipfile`, hence it's faster than what I described above. It can however be compared to checking if all packages are installed with `pip`:

```
root@9f6ecaf12cf8:/test# pip freeze > requirements.txt
root@9f6ecaf12cf8:/test# pip install -r requirements.txt
[...]
real	0m1.836s
user	0m1.610s
sys	0m0.130s
```

So according to this non-scientific test, Pipenv is taking 36 times as long and using 94 times more CPU than `pip`.

I know that there's a big difference between what's going on under the hood, but my point here is that the vastly longer time and resource usage may be a deal breaker for some with lots of dependencies.

While digging into this, I noticed that Pipenv is spawning one `pip` process for each package, and I wonder how much of a slowdown that is compared to `pip` doing everything inside one process. Would it potentially make sense to split the list of dependencies into 16 (or whatever `PIPENV_MAX_SUBPROCESS` is set to), in order to avoid having to spawn 212 `pip` processes - like it's the case here?

It might also be that this is all down to `pip` and trying to make it faster for the operations that Pipenv runs. I just thought I would start here and see if there perhaps could be some possible optimisations on the Pipenv side of things.

issue_title: `--extra-index-url` does not work with `pipenv install`
issue_body:
Only way to install package on private index (specifically one which has public subdependencies on PyPI) is to "manually" fill the "Pipfile" and do `pipenv install` [as suggested here](https://github.com/pypa/pipenv/pull/222). 

In other words, `pipenv install pkgname --extra-index-url <link-to-index>` doesn't work (neither does `pipenv install pkgname -i <link-to-index>` (as it shouldn't since `-i` corresponds to `--index-url` which is not desirable in this context.)

##### Describe your environment

1. OS Type: windows 7
1. Python version: `$ python -V` 2.7
1. Pipenv version: `$ pipenv --version` 9.0.3

##### Expected result

I expect for package to be installed from the private index, and its sub-dependencies (specified in its `setup.py`) be installed from the public index (PyPI). Ideally the additional `[[sources]]` paragraph would be added to the Pipefile and the private package's "index" key would point to this "extra-index-url" as its source. 

I understand that this service just may not be provided by `pipenv` (yet?), but I wonder if there *is* some existing solution that i'm not finding. 

##### Actual result

pipenv can't find the private package to collect/install it.  This is using either the `-i` or `--extra-index-url` as mentioned above. 

```
Error:  An error occurred while installing pkgname!
  Could not find a version that satisfies the requirement pkgname (from versions: )
No matching distribution found for pkgname
```

##### Steps to replicate

* Create package called pkgname. 
* Use `install_requires` in its `setup.py` to specify some public package as a dependency (e.g., `pandas`) 
* Upload it to private index. 
* Try to install it with `pipenv`

 `pipenv install pkgname --extra-index-url <link-to-index>`

issue_title: ResourceManager for Non-Package Data Files is not working as described in the documentation
issue_body:
I want to install a data file which is in my repository root when installing my package. I don't want to install it in a specific directory. It's a list of contributors which is loaded by an about dialog. So this file is not a package data file.

My setup.py:

    setup(
        ...,
        packages = find_packages('src'),
        data_files = [
            ('share/myproject', ['CONTRIBUTORS.txt'])
        ])

The file is installed in ``$prefix/share/myproject``. The actual path is irrelevant for me.

The documentation tells me:

    The data files can also be accessed using the ResourceManager API, 
    by specifying a Requirement instead of a package name

    from pkg_resources import Requirement, resource_filename
    filename = resource_filename(Requirement.parse("myproject"),"share/myproject/CONTRIBUTORS.txt")

This returns a path relative to the ``site-packages`` directory. When installed in developer mode with ``pip install --user -e .`` this is relative to the ``src`` directory (from the ``find_packages`` call?). Both paths are not pointing to the correct file as they should if the documentation is correct.

If the ``data_file`` option is a supported feature then either the documentation is wrong or the ResourceManager is using the wrong path. If this is not supported any more then it needs to be removed from the documentation.

issue_title: Implement ./setup.py install --pip
issue_body:
Back in December I proposed adding an opt-in `--pip` option to `./setup.py install` that will force using pip to install a package, rather than using `easy_install`.  This would address existing requests to stop using `easy_install` by default for packages that use setuptools, without breaking backward compatibility where needed.  The full proposal is here: https://mail.python.org/pipermail/distutils-sig/2015-December/027900.html

Individual projects can enable pip-installation by default by setting 
```
[install]
pip = True
```
in their setup.cfg.  Users may also install all projects with pip by default by setting the same in their pydistutils.cfg or equivalent.  Of course, we should be discouraging `./setup.py install` in the first place, but the idiom is stubbornly persistent in the wider Python universe.  (A global setting would be somewhat dangerous as there are still some packages that won't install from pip at all, but they are less common).

It hasn't been discussed really since then that I can see, but had positive feedback at the time.  I just didn't follow through with it due to the distraction of moving to a new country :)

The sample implementation I provided should be a good starting point.  Still to be resolved is the issue of using pip for `setup_requires` as well, and will take a little more work.

I just wanted to post an issue so that the idea isn't lost.  I intend to post a PR with tests soon.

issue_title: Wrapping does not work when string contains newline
issue_body:
Hello,

I made the observation, that wrapping does not work correctly, when the message string contains an explicit newline character ('\n').
Interestingly I was not able to replicated this false behavior by writing a new test (https://github.com/staeff/i18ndude/commit/8358f1240a3ac3db0d2b0230b8f8f903c95b9a4e). So the problem seems not to occur in `wrap_string` in `utils.py` (https://github.com/collective/i18ndude/blob/master/src/i18ndude/utils.py#L99) but somewhere else along the way.

To replicate the false behavior you could run the following steps. You should see the problem by having a look at the output of the git diff.

```bash
$ git clone https://gist.github.com/b9adc20c725521516318.git i18ndudetest
$ cd i18ndudetest
$ i18ndude sync --wrap --pot test.pot test.po  # i18ndude needs to be in $PATH
$ git diff
```
I would be willing to investigate this problem further, hints where to look for the error are appreciated.

issue_title: Parsing invalid icalendars fails without any warning
issue_body:
Example:

```python
import icalendar

event_str = """
BEGIN:VCALENDAR
BEGIN:VEVENT
DTEND:20150905T100000Z
DTSTART:20150905T090000Z
X-APPLE-RADIUS=49.91307046514149
UID:123
END:VEVENT
END:VCALENDAR
"""

calendar = icalendar.Calendar.from_ical(event_str)
print(calendar.to_ical())
```
returns
```
BEGIN:VCALENDAR
END:VCALENDAR
```

but probably should throw an exception or at least a warning instead of ignoring the invalid VEVENT silently. Also see https://github.com/geier/khal/issues/294 .
