issue_title: Tensordot broken
Memory leak in dot gradient
issue_body:
For a simple function


```
import autograd.numpy as np
from autograd import grad
from memory_profiler import memory_usage

def F(x,z):
    for i in range(100):
        z = np.dot(x,z)
    return np.sum(z)

dF = grad(F)
def m():
    from time import sleep
    x,z,n = make_data()
    gx = dF(x,z,n=n)
    sleep(0.1)
    return gx

mem_usage = np.array(memory_usage(m,interval=0.01))
mem_usage -= mem_usage[0]
```

and a manual gradient of the same function

```
def grad_dot_A(g,A,B):
    ga = np.dot(g,B.T)
    ga = np.reshape(ga,np.shape(A))
    return ga

def grad_dot_B(g,A,B):
    gb = np.dot(A.T,g)
    gb = np.reshape(gb, np.shape(B))
    return gb

def dF(x, init_z=10, n=100):
    z_stack = []
    z = init_z

    for i in list(range(n)):
        z_stack.append(z)
        z = np.dot(x, z)
    retval = np.sum(z)

    # Begin Backward Pass
    g_retval = 1
    g_x = 0

    # Reverse of: retval = np.sum(z)
    g_z = repeat_to_match_shape(g_out__0, z)
    for i in reversed(list(range(n))):

        # Reverse of: z = np.dot(x, z)
        z = z_stack.pop()
        tmp_g0 = grad_dot_A(g_z, x, z)
        tmp_g1 = grad_dot_B(g_z, x, z)
        g_z = 0
        g_x += tmp_g0
        g_z += tmp_g1
    return g_x
```

I get the following memory usage profile:

![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/161935/25681799/9fed2d90-3023-11e7-963d-ea413baf8797.png)

If I replace the dot gradient with the ones used in the manual code, I get the same memory profile, nothing improves. 

If I replace the dot product with element-wise multiply, I get a different memory profile:

![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/161935/25681971/5b6f35d6-3024-11e7-96ea-3fac8289e95b.png)

I would love to help figure this out, but I'm not sure where to start. First thing is of course to document the problem. I mentioned this problem in #199 , but I think it should be an issue.

issue_title: httpok doesnt work if timeout is < 10
issue_body:
This sounds unlikely but seems to be the case:

This commit: https://github.com/Supervisor/superlance/commit/0e6fb2d04a5eb144a2190e61745b6c915ded57e8#diff-d3d6eafafd2e31ce38ebab9e08f156eaL180

changed the logic for the retry loop. retry_time is hardcoded to 10 in httpok, so if timeout is < 10,
`range(self.timeout // (self.retry_time or 1) - 1 ,  -1, -1):`
becomes
`range(5 // 10 - 1 ,  -1, -1):`
...
`range(-1 ,  -1, -1):` 
which will never execute because it is `[]`.

I attempted to write a test case but I'm not really skilled enough to dissect the http_ok test file.

issue_title: Move heap cnt to stream
issue_body:
On the send since, the heap cnt is a properly of the heap. This makes it painful to send a heap on multiple streams - the cnts need to be kept synchronised. If it were removed from the heap and tracked internally by the stream, things would be simpler.

This would be a backwards-incompatible change.

issue_title: Support multiple endpoints in a udp_ibv_reader
issue_body:
Currently receiving to multiple endpoints requires either multiple streams (if they're logically separate) or multiple readers. Either way, it requires multiple threads or interrupt-driven operation. That is more scalable, but may be undesirable if a single core can handle the total rate and the other cores have other work to do.

An alternative is to have a single reader accept multiple endpoints, and attach flow rules for all of them to the QP. An additional advantage is that packets should then (in theory) be processed in the order they arrive at the NIC.

issue_title: Dependency on `six` can result in broken underlying SDK install, `pip` incorrectly deduces that requirements are satisfied
issue_body:
Technically we've done nothing wrong here, but we still might want to fix this.

Try the following steps:
```shell
$ virtualenv venv
$ soure venv/bin/activate
$ pip install six==1.0.0
$ pip install globus-cli
```

The CLI install finishes, but the results are bad. Because `globus-cli` depends on `six>=1.0.0,<2.0.0`, `pip` deduces that there's no need to upgrade that dependency.
However, `globus-sdk` depends on `six>=1.10.0,<2.0.0` because of some additions to `six.moves`, so the CLI crashes with an `ImportError` during startup.

`pip` should identify the requirement and either upgrade (as it would if we were installing `globus-sdk` into the virtualenv directly), or fail.

This whole state of affairs is irksome. It seems that we should maybe specify `six>=1.10.0,<2.0.0` in the CLI requirements(?).

issue_title: Creating directories along with their parents
issue_body:
Hello,

I've been trying to find a way to create a directory with its parent directories, in case they are missing.

My first thought was to use "operation_mkdir" of the TransferClient, but there is no option for creating parent directories (https://docs.globus.org/api/transfer/file_operations/#make_directory).
Another way could be via doing a dummy transfer with same source and destination, because a transfer task creates parent directories. For example I was thinking to do a transfer from "/dev/null" to ".dummy". However access to /dev/null is prohibited at some endpoints (eg. NERSC-DTN).
The last thing that comes to mind, is to loop over the path and create the parent directories one by one with "operation_mkdir". However this is too naive and slow. There should be another way...

Is there a designated way of creating directories along with their parents, when they don't exist?

Thanks,
George

issue_title: Make testsuite concurrency-safe
issue_body:
This is a known problem discussed partly in #120 
Many thanks to @aaschaer for his work on the testsuite (!), but he noted that there are issues with concurrency safety.

We should rectify this.

A couple of things we can do to start:
- [x] Normal teardown should only delete assets created during *this* test run
- [x] Tests should track all assets that are created and delete them all on teardown
- [x] Complete cleanup (e.g. delete all endpoints belonging to user) should be a manual task runnable from the repo, just like generating a new refresh token
- [x] Tests which use shared resources (e.x. Tutorial Endpoints) should insert random, unique strings into any identifiers used

This will get us into a better state, and we can attack any remaining issues as they arise.

issue_title: Argument order for oauth2_start_flow() differs between NativeApp and ConfidentialApp clients
issue_body:
I'm not sure if this can be fixed without breaking backwards-compatibility, so maybe not worth caring about. The method signature for `oauth2_start_flow()` differs between the [`ConfidentialAppAuthClient`](https://github.com/globus/globus-sdk-python/blob/master/globus_sdk/auth/client_types/confidential_client.py#L86) and [`NativeAppAuthClient`](https://github.com/globus/globus-sdk-python/blob/master/globus_sdk/auth/client_types/native_client.py#L40)

For confidential clients it's:

```python
    def oauth2_start_flow(
       self, redirect_uri, requested_scopes=None,
       state='_default', refresh_tokens=False):
```

For native app it's:

```python
    def oauth2_start_flow(
        self, requested_scopes=None, redirect_uri=None,
        state='_default', verifier=None, refresh_tokens=False,
        prefill_named_grant=None):
```
Obviously the native app version offers some extra parameters, but it would be very slightly nicer if the first four arguments to both methods were the same, in the same order.

Most people will be writing one or the other type of client, so the only people who could notice and get tripped up by this are users writing both types of clients.

issue_title: Support for tensorflow dynamic_rnn
issue_body:
Currently tflearn doesn't use `tensorflow tf.nn.dynamic_rnn` and `tf.nn.bidirectional_dynamic_rnn` for sequences with dynamic lengths.

Although there is a support for dynamic lengths by using `dynamic=True` with `tflearn.layers.recurrent.simple_rnn` (or similiar rnns) - as seen in #110 - which allows correct usage for varied-length sequences, under the hood it still uses `tensorflow.python.ops.nn.rnn` which statically creates the RNN graph.

While this works correctly, it doesn't benefit from the _major_ performance improvement of dynamic_rnn, and this could be a great future for tflearn.

Also, when trying to use tensorflow's dynamic_rnn directly, there is a problem with the control dependency in the `trainer.py` file, which caused me the error:

`tensorflow.python.framework.errors_impl.InvalidArgumentError: The node 'Adam/gradients/RNN/while/LSTMCell/add_grad/BroadcastGradientArgs/StackPush' has inputs from different frames. The input 'Adam/gradients/RNN/while/LSTMCell/add_grad/Shape' is in frame ''. The input 'Adam/gradients/RNN/while/LSTMCell/add_grad/BroadcastGradientArgs/RefEnter' is in frame 'RNN/while/RNN/while/'.`

(I made a temporary fix by loosing the dependency following dependency:
`            with tf.control_dependencies([loss_avg_op, acc_avg_op]):
                self.grad = tf.gradients(total_loss, self.train_vars)`
)

which causes the upload code using put_object fail when running under python 3.5 with invalid signature error. Sha256 hex string contains "r'" and "'" (sic). It makes signatures computed and sent different.
The line shall be:

`data_sha256_hex = encode_to_hex(get_sha256(current_data)).decode('ascii')`

As I went with other solution to my problems, I don't have time to fully fix this issue (as it seems that in some places bytes are used; in some - strings - I'd has to be a more systematic fix to support python3 IMHO).

issue_title: Ajtl a
Ctrl + Enter should hide the code for a Markdown cell (not just Shift + Enter)
issue_body:
If you are editing a Markdown cell, you see both the text that you are typing, as well as the Markdown rendering of that text. When you press `Shift + Enter`, the text entry part of the cell closes, and all you are left with is the rendered markdown.

However, `Shift + Enter` also creates another cell below, which is not always desirable. This is especially noticeable when you have a Markdown cell in the middle of the document. The only way to hide the edit box is to use `Shift + Enter`, and then manually delete the extra cell that was created.

I believe that `Ctrl + Enter` should have a similar behavior, as far as closing the edit box in a markdown cell, and leaving only the rendered Markdown... without the extra line being inserted.

issue_title: MILP OPF objective function value
issue_body:
For MILP problems the lower bound of the problem is the relaxed/best bound, at least in CPLEX. Thus the objective value of network.results["Problem"][0]["Lower bound"] for unit commitment problems is lower than the integer objective value. For MILP problems network.results["Problem"][0]["Upper bound"] can retrieve the correct value.
This can be verified in the unit-commitment test file by disabling any problem presolve and introducing a large enough optimality gap tolerance, in order to generate different upper and lower bounds. e.g. for CPLEX:

nu.lopf(nu.snapshots, solver_name='cplex', solver_options={'mip_tolerances_mipgap': 0.5,'preprocessing_presolve':'no'})

With tee=True in the opf.py file there will be a difference in the upper and lower bounds.

[opf.txt](https://github.com/FRESNA/PyPSA/files/1246265/opf.txt)
[unit-commitment.txt](https://github.com/FRESNA/PyPSA/files/1246266/unit-commitment.txt)

issue_title: An option to hide stdout / stderr from actions
issue_body: Sometimes we run into software packages (particularly in R) where the authors prints a lot runtime info on the screen without using a verbosity control to moderate it. It is straightforward to redirect the output in `run` / `sh` action but not with `R` or `python`. It can be very annoying particularly for some numerical algorithm that dumps large amount of information on the screen at each iteration. It also makes execution extremely slow. Does it sound reasonable that we add a switch to `sos run` to redirect stderr/stdout to /dev/null?

issue_title: Thoughts on paths configuration in hosts.yml
issue_body:
Right now we require all hosts having the same set of paths to construct `path_map`. There are two possible improvements:

1. Create `path_map` only for matched `paths` and ignore others. That is to say, if a local machine only with `home` if mapped to another machine with `home` and `projects`, the `path_map` would be local to remote `home`. The reverse can trigger an error because files under `projects` cannot be mapped.

2. Disallow paths outside of `paths` to make `paths` and `export` item which makes sense because SoS supposedly does not know how to map paths outside defined `paths` definition to remote host. The current behavior works like a default root to root map that would be used when other maps fail.

issue_title: Python wrapper required libraries
issue_body:
Hello, I installed Realsense sdk in Ubuntu 16.04 following packages installation (not from source).
When I tried to install the python wrapper from source as described [here](https://github.com/IntelRealSense/librealsense/tree/master/wrappers/python) I got some errors.

You should please add these steps to installation guide to succesfully install Python wrapper, even if you didn't install Realsense from source:
```
sudo apt-get install libusb-1.0-0-dev
sudo apt-get install libglfw3-dev
```

issue_title: skimage.transform.warp silently maps uint8 images to float
issue_body:
skimage.transform.warp silently maps uint8 images to float.

```python
tform = SimilarityTransform()
warp(127*np.ones((3,3), np.uint8), tform
```
```
array([[ 0.49803922,  0.49803922,  0.49803922],
       [ 0.49803922,  0.49803922,  0.49803922],
       [ 0.49803922,  0.49803922,  0.49803922]])
```

There is a flag `preserve_range`, but that does not quite do what is desired:
1. Type still converted to float
2. Range not capped in output at 0-255.

- [x] Python version
2.7
- [x] scikit-image version (run `skimage.__version__`)
`0.12.3`

issue_title: Django 2.0 support
issue_body:
django-private-storage 1.2.3 is incompatible with Django 2.0 yet, while Django 2.0 already exists in some repo files it can mislead some users.

Specific incompatibilities I've found:
 - in `permissions.py`
> Using User.is_authenticated() and User.is_anonymous() as methods rather than properties is no longer supported.
- in `fields.py`
> FileField methods get_directory_name() and get_filename() are removed.

I could miss something else.

Hope you're going to add support of Django 2.0 soon.

issue_title: Support AWS CodeBuild projects
issue_body:
Hello, thanks for the great work at CodeCov. We use it in several projects, and have recently ported from CircleCI to AWS CodeBuild (for price and AWS CodePipeline integration).

We are currently using the codecov shell script and doing some shell magic to make it all work. It would be wonderful if CodeBuild was supported "automagically" like the other CI providers.

If it helps, here is what we've done so far, and it seems to mostly be working. Once we pull the plug on Circle completely, we'll know if the uploads to master are working correctly.

`VCS_PULL_REQUEST=$(echo $CODEBUILD_SOURCE_VERSION | grep pr | sed 's/pr\///g')`

With that set, the git fallback seems to be handling the rest.

Here are the CodeBuild environment variables:
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/codebuild/latest/userguide/build-env-ref-env-vars.html

Thanks!

Hello,

DHCPv6 packets contaning valid User Class option (15) are not taken into account.   
2016-04-30 00:09:55,740 [MainThread] root#684 [WARNING] Invalid message from fe80::964a:cff:feb0:1a8e%eth0: Invalid packet from ('fe80::964a:cff:feb0:1a8e%eth0', 546, 0, 14)
After some investigations I was able to fix the issue on class UserClassOption(Option):
On load_from function after getting the size of the user class (user_class_length = unpack_from('!H', buffer, offset=offset + my_offset)[0]) we have to increase my_offset by 2 (user-class-len) to get the offset of opaque-data. 
----> This command (my_offset += 2) should be inserted on line 1792 just before getting the user_class. (options.py)

Regards,
Amine.

issue_title: Init of created genesis block fails
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

Attempting to create a new network using the tools provided in Raiden results in an invalid genesis json file.  Specifically, there appear to be three issues.  The first is that values for the "code" key should start with "0x" but do not.  For example:

    $ python tools/config_builder.py full_genesis > mycustomgenesis.json
    $ geth --datadir /tmp/privategeth init mycustomgenesis.json
    Fatal: invalid genesis file: missing 0x prefix for hex data
    $

This first issue can be overcome (as a hack) with a sed command, but this runs in to a second issue:

    $ python tools/config_builder.py full_genesis > mycustomgenesis.json
    $ sed -e 's/"code": "/"code": "0x/g' mycustomgenesis.json >mycustomgenesis2.json
    $ geth --datadir /tmp/privategeth init mycustomgenesis2.json
    Fatal: invalid genesis file: hex string has length 2, want 64 for Hash
    $

I'm unsure of exactly what is causing the second issue, but it looks like it is due to the key for the  "storage" entries created by the smart contracts being addresses whereas geth expects them to be hashes, for example

    $ cat mycustomgenesis2.json | jq '.alloc[].storage | select(length > 0)'
    {
      "0x06": "0x48302e3100000000000000000000000000000000000000000000000000000008"
    }
    $

Although not a fix, the fact that this is the issue can be shown by removing the storage objects from the genesis JSON:

    $ python tools/config_builder.py full_genesis > mycustomgenesis.json
    $ sed -e 's/"code": "/"code": "0x/g' mycustomgenesis.json >mycustomgenesis2.json
    $ cat mycustomgenesis2.json | jq -c 'del(.alloc[].storage)' >mycustomgenesis3.json
    $ geth --datadir /tmp/privategeth init mycustomgenesis3.json
    Fatal: invalid genesis file: missing 0x prefix for hex data
    $

The third issue is that the 'extraData' field in mk_genesis of tools/genesis_builder.py isn't hex-encoded.  The line

    genesis['extraData'] = CLUSTER_NAME

should be

    genesis['extraData'] = '0x' + CLUSTER_NAME.encode('hex')

At which point the chain can be created:

    $ python tools/config_builder.py full_genesis > mycustomgenesis.json
    $ sed -e 's/"code": "/"code": "0x/g' mycustomgenesis.json >mycustomgenesis2.json
    $ cat mycustomgenesis2.json | jq -c 'del(.alloc[].storage)' >mycustomgenesis3.json
    $ geth --datadir /tmp/privategeth init mycustomgenesis3.json
    WARN [05-25|12:36:10] No etherbase set and no accounts found as default 
    INFO [05-25|12:36:10] Allocated cache and file handles         database=/tmp/privategeth/geth/chaindata cache=16 handles=16
    INFO [05-25|12:36:10] Successfully wrote genesis state         database=chaindata                       hash=a523ae…454e0c
    INFO [05-25|12:36:10] Allocated cache and file handles         database=/tmp/privategeth/geth/lightchaindata cache=16 handles=16
    INFO [05-25|12:36:10] Successfully wrote genesis state         database=lightchaindata                       hash=a523ae…454e0c
    $

## Tasklist

- [ ] Ensure that "code" values are written as correct hex starting with '0x'
- [ ] Ensure that storage keys and values are written in a way suitable for geth to import
- [ ] Merge pending PR to fix creation of 'extraData' field when creating the genesis json

### System Description

Raiden from master
geth 1.6.1-stable-021c3c28 running on linux (also tested with master 1.6.2-unstable-b596b4ba)
All Python requirements taken from requirements.txt / requirements-dev.txt

issue_title: Raiden architecture
issue_body:
Use a declarative architecture similar to elm/redux to facilitate raiden testing and recoverability (#205).

#### Requisites:

- Use a stateless / referential transparant state machine.
  - This guarantees that all the state required to restart the node will be contained outside of the state machine.
  - The state machine is just a function that receives the current state and a new state transition to be applied, the result is a **new** state.
- Use a declarative approach based for state transitions of the state machine.
  - This allows for state transitions to be journaled in a WAL.
  - Protocol messages and blockchain events should be encoded as events.
- The result actions from the state machine allows the node to communicate with the external world.
  - Messages sent through the network or events used to notify the node user.
  - The state machine **cannot** produce actions that result in new state transitions, this remove the possibility of infinite loops on the handling of events.

#### TODOs:

- Make the mediated transfer a state machine.
  - Declare a state machine per kind of role in a mediated transfer 
    - [ ] initiator
    - [ ] mediator
    - [ ] target
  - Declare the valid protocol messages for each role as a state transition.
  - Declare the blockchain events relevant for each role as a state transition.
- Code the rules of a asset_swap into a state machine
  - Declare a state machine per kind of role in a mediated transfer 
    - [ ] maker
    - [ ] taker
  - Declare the valid protocol messages for each role as a state transition.
  - Declare the blockchain events relevant for each role as a state transition.
- Declare the result events from the state transitions

issue_title: Add AlpineLinux support
Part-select addressing
issue_body:
Hello,

I looked in the verilog standard ieee 1364-2005 regarding the partselect adressing. I've created a module base on their example.

```
module partselect_test
   (input wire [15:0] big_vect,
    input wire [0:15] little_vect,
 
    output wire [7:0] big_part1,
    output wire [7:0] big_part2,
 
    output wire [0:7] little_part1,
    output wire [0:7] little_part2);
 
    assign big_part1 = big_vect[ 0 +: 8]; // == big_vect[ 7 : 0]
    assign big_part2 = big_vect[15 -: 8]; // == big_vect[15 : 8]
 
    assign little_part1 = little_vect[ 0 +: 8]; // == little_vect[0 :  7]
    assign little_part2 = little_vect[15 -: 8]; // == little_vect[8 : 15]
 
endmodule
```

When I parsed the code, I obtained the following AST (I've put only the assign nodes) :

```
      Assign:  (at 11)
        Lvalue:  (at 11)
          Identifier: big_part1 (at 11)
        Rvalue:  (at 11)
          Partselect:  (at 11)                   // The partselect parsed is [0:8]
            Identifier: big_vect (at 11)         // The standard says [7:0]
            IntConst: 0 (at 11)
            Plus:  (at 11)
              IntConst: 0 (at 11)
              IntConst: 8 (at 11)
      Assign:  (at 12)
        Lvalue:  (at 12)
          Identifier: big_part2 (at 12)
        Rvalue:  (at 12)
          Partselect:  (at 12)                     // The partselect parsed is [15:7]
            Identifier: big_vect (at 12)           // The standard says [15:8]
            IntConst: 15 (at 12)
            Minus:  (at 12)
              IntConst: 15 (at 12)
              IntConst: 8 (at 12)
      Assign:  (at 14)
        Lvalue:  (at 14)
          Identifier: little_part1 (at 14)
        Rvalue:  (at 14)
          Partselect:  (at 14)                     // The partselect parsed is [0:8]
            Identifier: little_vect (at 14)        // The standard says [0:7]
            IntConst: 0 (at 14)
            Plus:  (at 14)
              IntConst: 0 (at 14)
              IntConst: 8 (at 14)
      Assign:  (at 15)
        Lvalue:  (at 15)
          Identifier: little_part2 (at 15)
        Rvalue:  (at 15)
          Partselect:  (at 15)                     // The partselect parsed is [15:7]
            Identifier: little_vect (at 15)        // The standard says [8:15]
            IntConst: 15 (at 15)
            Minus:  (at 15)
              IntConst: 15 (at 15)        
              IntConst: 8 (at 15)
```

I think that you cannot resolve the "+:" or "-:" without analyzing the declaration of the variable to know if the bit range is ascending or descending (see parser.py lines 726, 731, 741, 746).

In my opinion the best way to manage this issue would be to create a new node type (PartselectDynamic for instance). This node would be instantiated by the parser when the tokens PLUSCOLON or MINUSCOLON are found. The codegen would be easy to modify, but I don't know the impact on the other parts of PyVerilog.

I can try this solution if you want.

Christophe

issue_title: Cython compilation
issue_body:
[setup.zip](https://github.com/natasha/yargy/files/1310189/setup.zip)
Добрый день! Для ускорения работы попробовал собрать вашу библиотеку с помощью cython. Если интересно, то прикрепляю setup.py файл, который использовал для этого.

Собрать не получилось без 2-х небольших правок (возможно это небольшие баги, которые стоит поправить):
1. В файле interpretation/fact.py отсутствует импорт `assert_type` из yargy.utils. И это очень похоже на баг =)
1. В файле rule/constructors.py сборка падала на строчке 196:
```self.rule = rule(item, *items)```. Тут я не уверен, что это баг, но замена `rule` на `Rule` помогла и сборка успешно прошла.

Однако, при попытке использовать полученную собранную либу вылетает ошибка:
> C:\Users\alexandr.romanenko\workspace\cython\yargy\parser.pyx in yargy.parser.Parser.__init__ (yargy\parser.c:6233)()
> 
> C:\Users\alexandr.romanenko\workspace\cython\yargy\rule\constructors.pyx in yargy.rule.constructors.Rule.normalized (yargy/rule\constructors.c:3524)()
> 
> C:\Users\alexandr.romanenko\workspace\cython\yargy\rule\constructors.pyx in yargy.rule.constructors.Rule.transform (yargy/rule\constructors.c:3311)()
> 
> C:\Users\alexandr.romanenko\workspace\cython\yargy\visitor.pyx in yargy.visitor.TransformatorsComposition.__call__ (yargy\visitor.c:1910)()
> 
> C:\Users\alexandr.romanenko\workspace\cython\yargy\rule\transformators.pyx in yargy.rule.transformators.RuleTransformator.__call__ (yargy/rule\transformators.c:1531)()
> 
> C:\Users\alexandr.romanenko\workspace\cython\yargy\rule\constructors.pyx in genexpr (yargy/rule\constructors.c:4227)()
> 
> ValueError: generator already executing

Ошибка возникает при попытке создать экземпляр класса Parser. При этом если пользоваться обычной python версией библиотеки, все работает и создается корректно

Понимаю, что вопрос довольно абстрактный, но возможно вы подскажите, что я сделал не так?
Возможно, правка 2 была не верна, и чтобы собрать yargy под cython нужны другие (возможно, более глобальные) исправления кода. Или может быть по этому небольшому сообщению об ошибке Вы все-таки поймете, в чем проблема... 
Все-таки получить 30% ускорения работы библиотеки просто за счет сборки cython - это очень заманчиво =) 

И еще вопрос: на данный момент `pip install yargy` устанавливает версию 0.8.0 библиотеки. Я правильно понимаю, что нужно устанавливать библиотеку прямо из github, забирая последнюю версию (0.9.1), потому как все примеры и туториалы на 0.8.0 не работают...

issue_title: Custom channel with HTTP auth broken in conda 4.2
issue_body:
Previously, it was possible to have a custom channel with basic HTTP auth using the `https://<user>:<pass>@<path>` syntax. With conda 4.2, this is not the case anymore. Instead, when such a channel is present in `condarc`, one gets 401 errors whenever trying to install or update something. I identified two reasons:
- Since `urllib3`'s `parse_url` is used instead of `urllib.parse.urlparse` (in common/url.py), the username and password are stripped from the `netloc` attribute and the information is therefore lost.
- Even if username and password are retained as part of the URL (e. g. by using `urllib.parse.urlparse` as I did for testing purposes), authentification is not successful, because (in connection.py) the `CondaSession` class sets `self.auth = BinstarAuth()`, apparently overriding the credentials given as part of the URL. If I remove the line, the HTTP auth works again.

Apart from that, #2804 is still true for conda 4.2.

issue_title: Unintentional build downgrades in mixed feature settings
issue_body:
Under certain limited circumstances involving features, `conda` unexpectedly downgrades to an earlier build of a package. The conditions are as follows:

- The current version of a package `foo` has multiple builds with distinct build numbers.
- Newer builds depend on a second package `bar`, while older builds do not.
- Some of the valid choices for the `bar` dependency have features, and some do not.

When those conditions are met, the command
```
conda create -n errtest foo
```
will select an older build of `foo`, without the `bar` dependency, instead of the latest build. (Contact me or @msarahan for a specific test case.)

Currently, this is not occurring with conda 4.4. The reasons for this are not fully clear, because the bug has been located and exists in 4.3 and 4.4. Bug fix coming.

issue_title: Add authentication property to `.condarc`
issue_body:
I been trying to set up a private conda channel on a private webserver that requires the user to authenticate for access. This is similar to [this](https://groups.google.com/a/continuum.io/forum/#!topic/anaconda/tLUFkHd7DDc) or [this](https://github.com/pypa/pip/issues/1182) from `pip`. 

A more generic solution is to allow the use of requests `auth` property to pass along method/library and parameters needed  to authenticate into the network. 
The `.condarc` file would look like:
```
Authenticate:
  library: requests-ntlm
  username: "Joe"
  password: "pass"
```
where `library` can be any python package that can be used by requests. The other fields will then be passed to the library as parameter when the `CondaSession` is created.

issue_title: conda search should tell you where packages from the public channel really come from
Selectively installing development versions
issue_body:
A discussion we have had at conda-forge is how to install development releases. See this issue ( https://github.com/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io/issues/147 ). There are some great benefits of being able to do something like this. The biggest ones are speeding up the release process and increased community engagement. We have tried this in one case. In particular, we have tried this with `matplotlib`. See this [comment]( https://github.com/conda-forge/matplotlib-feedstock/pull/24#issuecomment-221496870 ) for a brief walkthrough of how it worked.

The biggest challenge we face ATM is that enabling the development channel results in getting development releases of everything. As can be imagined, this is suboptimal. Most people would want to enable one type of dev release selectively so they can try that one package. One solution would be to name the dev channels distinctly, but having so many dev channels would quickly be a management nightmare for the end user. It would be great if we can start a larger discussion about how `conda` can facilitate this. Also it would be helpful if we think about some concrete changes in conda that would make this possible.

I have cc'd everyone part of this discussion at conda-forge in hopes that we can work with Continuum to find a healthy solution to this important problem.

cc @pelson @ocefpaf @astrofrog @Cadair @jjhelmus @rgommers @ChrisBarker-NOAA @patricksnape @janschulz @SylvainCorlay @tacaswell @msarahan @mcg1969 @kalefranz

issue_title: Why need PrefixReplaceLinkAction? It breaks Windows permissions.
issue_body:
**I'm submitting a...** - [x] bug report - [ ] feature request ## Current Behavior If the conda environment is read-only to Users, installing as admin packages with files that have "prefix_placeholder" overwrite these files permissions to have no access to Users and makes these packages unusable. ### Steps to Reproduce 1. There is a base installation of Anaconda in C:\Anaconda with permissions inherited from C:\: * Administrators read/write * Users read-only 2. However, C:\Anaconda\Library\Qt5Core.dll permissions are different and explicit: * Administrators read/write * Administrator user read/write * Users - no access 3. `conda uninstall qt` and `conda install qt` as administrator user do not change anything 4. Upon further investigation it turns out that in C:\Anaconda\pkgs\qt-5.9.6-vc14h62aca36_0\info\paths.json the entry for Qt5Core.dll has prefix placeholder: ``` { "_path": "Library/bin/Qt5Core.dll", "file_mode": "binary", "path_type": "hardlink", "prefix_placeholder": "C:/qt5/_h_env", "sha256": "50886da211b4acaacc015e18f52fe44c2a07500f05fd09a9cb3a5c026a5eaba4", "size_in_bytes": 5241344 }, ``` 5. The TRACE output of `conda install qt` shows that in this case PrefixReplaceLinkAction is used: ``` TRACE conda.core.path_actions:verify(385): copying C:\Anaconda\pkgs\qt-5.9.6-vc14h62aca36_0\Library\bin\Qt5Core.dll => C:\Users\labdsvm-admin\tmp6f8xpxgo\a39281e8-cb0c-4f01-9d32-e22b2209f393 TRACE conda.gateways.disk.create:_do_copy(275): copying C:\Anaconda\pkgs\qt-5.9.6-vc14h62aca36_0\Library\bin\Qt5Core.dll => C:\Users\labdsvm-admin\tmp6f8xpxgo\a39281e8-cb0c-4f01-9d32-e22b2209f393 TRACE conda.core.path_actions:verify(390): rewriting prefixes in C:\Anaconda\Library\bin\Qt5Core.dll ``` 6. The temp directory is created by mkdtemp() and the [documentation](https://docs.python.org/3/library/tempfile.html#tempfile.mkdtemp) says that > The directory is readable, writable, and searchable only by the creating user ID. 7. This operation first copies the file from pkgs folder to temporary folder that is only writable by current user and later it hardlinks the file from this directory into C:\Anaconda\Library\bin. ``` TRACE conda.core.path_actions:execute(415): linking C:\Users\labdsvm-admin\tmp6f8xpxgo\a39281e8-cb0c-4f01-9d32-e22b2209f393 => C:\Anaconda\Library\bin\Qt5Core.dll TRACE conda.gateways.disk.create:create_link(321): hard linking C:\Users\labdsvm-admin\tmp6f8xpxgo\a39281e8-cb0c-4f01-9d32-e22b2209f393 => C:\Anaconda\Library\bin\Qt5Core.dll ``` 8. The hardlinks retain source file's permissions, so now, the DLL is not readable by other Users. ## Expected Behavior I do not understand why the treatment for files with prefix_placeholder is so radically different. Why conda cannot hardlink the file from pkgs folder as other files? Why copy into temp folder with specifically minimal permissions? The workaround could be to not use system temp folder, but create a temp folder in conda pkgs directory so that permissions are retained. ## Environment Information
`conda info`

``` C:\Users\labdsvm-admin>conda info active environment : None user config file : C:\Users\labdsvm-admin\.condarc populated config files : conda version : 4.5.5 conda-build version : 3.10.9 python version : 3.6.4.final.0 base environment : C:\Anaconda (writable) channel URLs : https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/main/win-64 https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/main/noarch https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/free/win-64 https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/free/noarch https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/r/win-64 https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/r/noarch https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/pro/win-64 https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/pro/noarch https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/msys2/win-64 https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/msys2/noarch package cache : C:\Anaconda\pkgs C:\Users\labdsvm-admin\AppData\Local\conda\conda\pkgs envs directories : C:\Anaconda\envs C:\Users\labdsvm-admin\AppData\Local\conda\conda\envs C:\Users\labdsvm-admin\.conda\envs platform : win-64 user-agent : conda/4.5.5 requests/2.18.4 CPython/3.6.4 Windows/10 Windows/10.0.14393 administrator : True netrc file : None offline mode : False ```

issue_title: SELECTs with explicit fields fail on sqlite with a "row value misused"
issue_body:
Pypika generates SQL like ``SELECT ("Col", "Name") FROM "Table"``, which trips up SQLite. SQLite seems to be happy with ``SELECT Col, Name FROM "Table"``, though.

This appears to be the problem, as the brackets are always added in: https://github.com/kayak/pypika/blob/master/pypika/queries.py#L744

issue_title: Changing on filenames in importing
issue_body: When i import my workspace from databricks, somes files has name altered. Usually the last letter missing.

issue_title: Abstract ObjectTypes don't have a specific type.
issue_body:
I have this error, when I add a second inheritance class in my main `Query`.
```
class Query(trainings.schema.Query, brainstormers.schema.Query):
    """This class will inherit from multiple Queries as we begin to add more apps to our project."""

    pass

schema = graphene.Schema(name='MTP Schema')
schema.query = Query
```

issue_title: Query with "or" expression
Saving null value for nullable fields
issue_body:
I had a problem saving the null values in the mutations. Is it possible to implement functionality to `get_argument_values` function could distinguish passed null value and not passed value?
I think it can be implemented similar to the `executor.ececute_fields` using Undefined.

issue_title: Cannot return null for non-nullable field Type.field.
issue_body:
If I create add a new field and the field is null, it leads to an error in
```Cannot return null for non-nullable field Type.field.```

In my model, I didn't set required to true for that field. After inspecting I found this in converter.py
```String(description=field.db_field, required=not field.null)```
which sets all field to required.

issue_title: Should enums have sub-selection of value, label and choices?
why should we specify input and output shape of DeConv2dLayer?
issue_body:
I think we only need to supply filter size and stride size and number of filters.

-1 is not supported in this layer, it is annoying.

And if I use DeConv2d, the output shape is not defined (it is ?), which makes the consecutive operation troublesome.

Why not just use `tf.contrib.layers.conv2d_transpose` ?

issue_title: Incorrect parameters for Partition.mount_iso_image()
issue_body:
There is a number of issues in the interface and implementation of the `Partition.mount_iso_image()` method:

* The method is currently defined to have input properties. However, the HMC operation 'Mount ISO Image' has no input properties that would be put into the request body, but instead the request body is the binary content of the image file.
* For this HMC operation, the `Content-Type` header must be set to `application/octet-stream` because the request body is binary data, but the current implementation does not set that header.
* This HMC operation has two query parameters (in the URL), which are not supported by the current interface and implementation of the method.

issue_title: sovrin_sign and sovrin_verify_signature should support arbitrary byte array
issue_body:
Right now, `sovrin_sign` and `sovrin_verify_signature` can create/verify signatures for the JSON messages that are sent to the ledger. In these methods, a JSON message is serialized to a byte array using a custom algorithm that is not used outside of Indy/Sovrin ledger communication. Also, the signature is automatically inserted into the JSON message.

I think it would be very useful if those methods could be used more generically, to create/verify signatures on any arbitrary byte array supplied by the library user.

issue_title: conversion scripts should accept long or short file paths
issue_body:
Conversion scripts like sio_magic should be able to take long or short file names.  I.e.:

    sio_magic.py -f infile.txt -WD path/to/

or

    sio_magic.py -f path/to/infile.txt

or, of course, if you're in the path/to directory:

    sio_magic.py -f infile.txt

To support this, I've added a pmag function resolve_file_name that takes the filename and the working directory (if available).  It prioritizes the long file name, then if that doesn't work tries to create a long file name with working directory + the filename, and if that fails create a long file name with "." + the filename.  

Once implemented, this should be more flexible _and_ less confusing.

issue_title: Validation of user defined formats only works for primitive schemas
issue_body:
Given:
```python
def validate_palindrome(x):
    if x != ''.join(reversed(x)):
        raise SwaggerValidationException('%s is not a palindrome' % x)

palindrome_format = SwaggerFormat(
    format='palindrome',
    to_wire=lambda x: x,
    to_pyton=lambda x: x,
    validate=validate_palindrome)
```
This works for schema:
```json
{
    "name": "foo",
    "type": "string",
    "format": "palindrome"
}
```
But doesn't work for nested primitives:
```json
{
    "type": "object",
    "properties": {
        "foo": {
            "type": "string", 
            "format": "palindrome"
        }
    }
}
```
This is because validation is outsourced wholesale to `Swagger20Validator` which is not aware of the registered `SwaggerFormat`s.  Few options here:
1. Figure out how to hook the SwaggerFormat validation functions into Swagger20Validator which in turn uses `jsonschema` (best)
2. Make another pass over the schema post-Swagger20Validator and find/validate every `format` found (ick, a hack!)
3. Post-pone the validation for user defined formats until unmarshalling and do it then (double ick, a nastier hack!)

beta = 1. * galsim.radians
gal.applyShear(g=g,beta=beta)

print 'using ground PSF'
pixel_scale = 0.2
fwhm = 0.65 
atmos = galsim.Moffat(beta=3,fwhm=fwhm)
optics = galsim.Airy(lam_over_diam = (700e-9/4)*(180/numpy.pi)*3600)

print 'getting hsm'
hsm_result = galsim.FindAdaptiveMom(image)
print 'hsm success E1=%2.3f E2=%2.3f' % (hsm_result.observed_shape.getE1(),hsm_result.observed_shape.getE2())
image.write('gal72615.ground.fits')

print 'using space PSF'
pixel_scale = 0.03
lam = 700.
diam = 1.3
psf = galsim.Airy(lam_over_diam=lam/diam/1.e9*206265.) 
pix = galsim.Pixel(pixel_scale)
final = galsim.Convolve([gal,psf,pix])
n_pix = 196
image = galsim.ImageD(n_pix,n_pix)
print 'drawing'
final.draw(image,dx=pixel_scale)
image.write('gal72615.space.fits')
print 'getting hsm'
hsm_result = galsim.FindAdaptiveMom(image)

issue_title: Event payload attributes on batch requests
issue_body:
> This is a follow-up bug of #942

On batch requests, an event is sent for each (parent id / resource / action), and the list of objects is provided in `event.impacted_records`. But the content of `event.payload` makes very little sense in this context, and can lead to serious confusions:

```
>>> event.payload

{ 'action': 'update',
  'bucket_id': u'staging',
  'collection_id': u'gfx',
  'record_id': u'fd6a9531-af10-79a0-e645-4249218dbf4f',
  'resource_name': 'record',
  'timestamp': 1479999631700L,
  'uri': u'/buckets/staging/collections/gfx/records/fd6a9531-af10-79a0-e645-4249218dbf4f',
  'user_id': 'basicauth:cbd3731f18c97ebe1d31d9846b5f1b95cf8eeeae586e201277263434041e99d1'}
```

I propose that when there are more than one impacted object then we drop the `${resource_name}_id` and the `uri` keys.

Thoughts?

    - Note whether it is to extend existing functionality or introduce new functionality
   
-->

I think that it would be great to have a command that shows lavalink status, so we could directly know if lavalink is running, on which ports... At least we could know why the bot is always returning "Not found" or that kind of things.

Also the command shouldn't be added under the audio cog, but without cog using `bot.add_command()` (so it will be shown in the "No category" section, but we can make it hidden). Why such a thing? Because the cog is directly linked to lavalink and sometimes, when it crash, the commands gets unloaded, so something independent from the audio cog would be great to know what's really going on!

Red needs a way to exist solely from a file passed to redbot rather than relying on an existing install, allowing a bypass of redbot-setup (and it's inter-activeness) for docker.

```python
import multiprocessing, time
import msgpackrpc

class SumServer(object):
    def notification(self, s):
        print(s)

def server():
    server = msgpackrpc.Server(SumServer())
    server.listen(msgpackrpc.Address("localhost", 4000))

def client():

def main():

    time.sleep(0.5)

    try:
        client()
    finally:
        proc_server.terminate()

if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()
```

Are there any examples of using `.notify()`?

issue_title: server fixtures TestServer doesn't kill on macOS
issue_body:
TestServer has
```
        # Wait for server to die.
        for _ in range(retries):
            netstat_cmd = ("netstat -anp 2>/dev/null | grep %s:%s | grep LISTEN | "
                           "awk '{ print $7 }' | cut -d'/' -f1" % (socket.gethostbyname(self.hostname), self.port))
            pids = [p.strip() for p in self.run(netstat_cmd, capture=True, cd='/').split('\n') if p.strip()]
```
But
```
$ netstat -anp
netstat: option requires an argument -- p
Usage:	netstat [-AaLlnW] [-f address_family | -p protocol]
	netstat [-gilns] [-f address_family]
	netstat -i | -I interface [-w wait] [-abdgRtS]
	netstat -s [-s] [-f address_family | -p protocol] [-w wait]
	netstat -i | -I interface -s [-f address_family | -p protocol]
	netstat -m [-m]
	netstat -r [-Aaln] [-f address_family]
	netstat -rs [-s]
```

It seems that it's expecting gnu netstat. The `2>/dev/null` masks the error output and because it's in a pipe, the exit status is that of `cut`, which is non-failure.

issue_title: Don't reset key sequence when setting the same master key for more than one time
issue_body: In current `SetMasterKey()` implementation, the `keysequence` will be reset without checking the set key is a new one or not.  It may cause some key index mismatch issue.

issue_title: Router floods network with Child Update Requests after reset
issue_body:
We see a suspicious behavior of a router/leader after reset. If the router had any children then, after reset, it will send a child Update Request to each of them. The issue is that it keeps doing so every second, for about 240 seconds (I guess until all children are removed from the child table). Is that an intended?

Steps to reproduce:
- start a device and wait until it promotes to a leader
- start another device and wait until it attaches as a child
- switch off the second device
- reset the first device and observe the issue

issue_title: Should IMDB dataset's path allowed to be None?

In case of not having the dataset, I fear that what ever puts in the `path` will be ignored. Since, when calling IMDB's `iters` method, it will rewrite the path as `root + 'train'`, for instance, by creating a new dataset, regardless of what `path` it currently has.

issue_title: MongoObserver crashes with non-serializable objects
issue_body:
The MongoObserver crashes when it tries to handle an object that is not serializable.
When writing to `_run.info` this is not a problem, but when this happens with the return value of the main function, the experiment is not finished and the status stays "RUNNING".

Code to reproduce:
```python
from sacred import Experiment
from sacred.observers import MongoObserver

class NonSerializable:
    pass

ex = Experiment('test')
ex.observers.append(MongoObserver())

@ex.automain
def main(_run):
    myObject = NonSerializable()
    _run.info["test_info"] = myObject
    return myObject
```

issue_title: webpush modifies the vapid_claims parameter
issue_body:
I have an object in my Django config for vapid claims and I'm using it in the webpush method. 

I couldn't realize why after sending push messages on Firefox I was constantly getting "unauthorized registration" errors on Chrome, until I saw that the object passed as `vapid_claims` parameter gets modified from something like this:

```
{
    'sub': 'mailto:<my_email>'
}
```

to this :
```
{
    'aud': 'https://updates.push.services.mozilla.com',
    'exp': '1508309139',
    'sub': 'mailto:<my_email>'
}
```
I tried passing every time a new object and everything works fine, but now I'm wondering, is this expected behavior, and am I supposed to store that modified object?

issue_title: Chrome --disable-gpu flag
issue_body:
Currently running a headless Chrome tests on a server, including TravisCI servers, require passing `--disable-gpu` to `chrome` process.  Otherwise you get error "exit code 1" from Selenium. As far as I know there is no published schedule when `--disable-gpu` goes away as promised by Chrome development team.

I suggest adding a flag to `chrome_options` to control this besides `--headless`. 

I can champion a PR to get this added.

Example where this should be added:

https://github.com/websauna/splinter/commit/607ab7c4386a3f5d4edc3f3d96aa42aa94def812

issue_title: Allow reading the status code
issue_body:
How to read status code? According to documentation only options are to check if response was successful or compare it with a value. When trying to print out the status code, only object representation is shown. Consider following:

```python
>>> browser.visit('/foobar')
>>> browser.status_code.status_code.is_success()
True
>>> browser.status_code == 200
False
>>> print browser.status_code
<splinter.request_handler.status_code.StatusCode object at 0xa435a6c>
>>> # Now what is the response code?
```

Change request: print out the human readable response instead.

issue_title: Drop bleach.encoding module
issue_body:
`bleach.encoding` is a 63 line file that has a single meaningful function that is used [once](https://github.com/mozilla/bleach/blob/16551353ef6edc00d48f81ded4b4b6e030f910ed/bleach/__init__.py#L104). The only time `force_unicode` is used is to ensure that the input to `clean` is `six.text_type` instead of bytes. It includes all sorts of [weird](https://github.com/mozilla/bleach/blob/16551353ef6edc00d48f81ded4b4b6e030f910ed/bleach/encoding.py#L55-L59), [unused](https://github.com/mozilla/bleach/blob/16551353ef6edc00d48f81ded4b4b6e030f910ed/bleach/encoding.py#L32) paths and handles old Django types that aren't even used by Django anymore.

I think the entire module could be replaced by using `six.text_type` on [`__init__.py:104`](https://github.com/mozilla/bleach/blob/16551353ef6edc00d48f81ded4b4b6e030f910ed/bleach/__init__.py#L104) (though `linkify()` doesn't have that call, so maybe linkify _should_, or maybe clean doesn't need it?).

issue_title: Incorrect Handling of Arrow Keys in miniterm.py
issue_body:
I stumpled upon this bug when tracing down a problem in mpfshell - a shell for micropython [1].
There the problem came up that the arrrow keys show a lagging behaviour when navigating the shell history or the command line.
Basically you press ARROW-UP, nothing happens, you press ARROW-UP again and get the arrow-up functionality once [2]. This, of course, renders the REPL of micropython pretty useless...

To find out what is received and sent by miniterm I added two lines of debugging code:

```
diff miniterm-backup.py miniterm.py
168a169
>             print("[def getkey] key list: ",list(c))
493a495
>                     print("[def writer] transformed: ",list(text), " encoded: ",list(self.tx_encoder.encode(text)))
```

This made clear that there is at least a bug in the getkey function (and probably in the encoding function)..

After the first keypress you get

```
[def getkey] key list:  ['\x1b']
[def writer] transformed:  ['\x1b']  encoded:  [27]
```

After the next single keypress you get

```
[def getkey] key list:  ['[']
[def writer] transformed:  ['[']  encoded:  [91]
[def getkey] key list:  ['A']
[def writer] transformed:  ['A']  encoded:  [65]
[def getkey] key list:  ['\x1b']
[def writer] transformed:  ['\x1b']  encoded:  [27]
```

Here getkey keeps kind of lagging behind. And I'm not sure about the correctness of the encoding...

The problem seems to be limited to Linux and Python 3.x. I've done the testing with Kubuntu 16.04-64,
Python 3.5.2 and pyserial 3.2.1.


GHPS

-------------
[1] https://github.com/wendlers/mpfshell
[2] https://github.com/wendlers/mpfshell/issues/11

issue_title: select query fails, if query contains '$' symbol
issue_body:
`list(db.select("SELECT value FROM $table WHERE key ='$utm_source'", model_class=CHEventProperty))`
raises Exception
```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/code.py", line 91, in runcode
    exec(code, self.locals)
  File "<input>", line 1, in <module>
  File "/home/sergei/venv36/lib/python3.6/site-packages/infi/clickhouse_orm/database.py", line 157, in select
    query = self._substitute(query, model_class)
  File "/home/sergei/venv36/lib/python3.6/site-packages/infi/clickhouse_orm/database.py", line 273, in _substitute
    query = Template(query).substitute(mapping)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/string.py", line 126, in substitute
    return self.pattern.sub(convert, self.template)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/string.py", line 119, in convert
    return str(mapping[named])
KeyError: 'utm_source'
```

The problem is `database._substitute()` method which uses `Template(query).substitute(mapping)`. Template expects all keys mentioned in query being in mapping. Suppose, [safe_substitute()](https://docs.python.org/2/library/string.html#string.Template.safe_substitute) method should be used here.

issue_title: Outdated statement about comment edit histories

https://github.com/dotnet/core/blob/master/Documentation/contributing/maintainers.md

> **Maintainers won't**

Transparency is a cornerstone of effective discourse and its absence undermines trust.

This was changed in May.

I'm not advocating that maintainers should or should not edit contributor comments, but this statement as it stands is no longer correct.

issue_title: JSON Comment Removal RE may remove comments from within JSON strings
issue_body:
(The regex used to remove comments)[https://github.com/scrapinghub/extruct/blob/c465e629c9e35cff08a703f6d2912c1c71c642ff/extruct/jsonld.py#L13] from JSON that fails to decode is pinned to the beginning on one side, but not the other. So, the regex may remove HTML comments from strings within the JSON document, as well as outside the JSON.

A quick fix would be to just add the `^` token to the other pattern, or to brace the two patterns to share the same `^`. But, I'm wondering whether trailing comments are also an occasional problem, and if so whether a custom little FSM scanner might be a better solution? E.g., something that scans for the earliest possible "valid" opening character for a JSON document, and the last such character, and returns the indices for those two characters for decoding?

issue_title: For date parsing, the time component is being cached between calls.
issue_body:
Maybe this is a "feature" but it smells more like a bug. Reporting in case, since its caused some headaches. 
Using dateparser 0.3.1

Expected behavior:
the "time" part of the datetime object should be the current time when parsing a value with no time info like `today`. 

Current behavior: 
When parsing `today`, if you call it again at a later time the time is being cached in between. Even if calling with a different value like `hoy` (today in spanish).
This is a bit surprising, and I haven't tested what would happen the time rolls over to a different date but I assume that could be problematic also.

```
Python 2.7.10 (default, Sep 30 2015, 17:12:08)
[GCC 4.2.1 Compatible Apple LLVM 7.0.0 (clang-700.0.72)] on darwin
Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.

issue_title: asyncio.wait() on zmq.asyncio.Poller.poll() took longer than 100ms
issue_body:
# Problem Statement
I am getting sporadic performance warnings from ```asyncio.wait()``` itself.
The application is a simple messaging loop running at ~150 msg/sec in a burst of 3000 msg in a PUB-SUB scenario with single subscriber. The loop is interruptable: it polls and also waits for an interrupt stop ```asyncio.Event()```, whichever comes first.

```
Executing <Task pending coro=<...> wait_for=<Future pending cb=[Task._wakeup()] created at /usr/lib64/python3.5/asyncio/base_events.py:252> cb=[finalize_task(<Task pending ...>)() at ...] created at ...> took 0.105 seconds
```

The issue seem to appear when the Task for ```zmq.asyncio.Poller.poll()``` is done. But...

- Adding a ```timeout=1.0``` to the ```asyncio.wait()``` call seem to eliminate the warning but results in the loop having to spin a few more times with everything in pending.
- Adding PYTHONASYNCIODEBUG=1 seems to also eliminate the warning.
- Adding a ```timeout``` to the ```zmq.asyncio.Poller.poll()``` DOES NOT eliminate the warning.
- In lower burst scenarios, the warning does not happen.
- Setting SNDHWM, SNDBUF, RCVHWM and RCVBUF to big values (6000 for HWM and 60,000,000 respectively BUF) DOES NOT eliminate the warning

# Prototype code snippet
It's hard to carve out a fully working simple example, so I hope this is enough:

```
[...] # Assume we started the loop, created a PUB-SUB and data is flowing. Inside the loop coroutine:
interrupt = asyncio.Event()
zmq_poller = zmq.asyncio.Poller()
fs = set()
fut_wait = None
fut_poll = None

while not interrupt.is_set():
    # Either poll or wait for interrupt
    if fut_wait is None:
        fut_wait = asyncio.ensure_future(interrupt.wait())
        fs.add(fut_wait)
    if fut_poll is None:
        fut_poll = asyncio.ensure_future(zmq_poller.poll())
        fs.add(fut_poll)
    done, pending = await asyncio.wait(fs, return_when=concurrent.futures.FIRST_COMPLETED)

    # What is done is done, what is pending we still wait for in the next loop iteration
    if fut_poll in done:
        items = dict(await fut_poll)
        if s in items and items[s] == poll_type:
            if await loop_action() == False:
                break
        fut_poll = None
    if fut_wait in done:
        await fut_wait
        fut_wait = None
    fs = pending
```

# Context
Python-3.5.2 (default, Oct 14 2016, 12:54:53) 
[GCC 6.2.1 20160916 (Red Hat 6.2.1-2)]
libzmq-4.1.4
pyzmq-15.3.0

issue_title: Broken Compatability with Python 2.7?
issue_body:
I noticed some recent commits that seem to break compatibility with Python 2.7. For example line 28 in `zmq/auth/asyncio.py`:

`events = yield from self.__poller.poll()`

It looks like "yield from" was only introduced in Python 3.3 as per https://docs.python.org/3/whatsnew/3.3.html .  One of the stated goals of PyZMQ is:

> PyZMQ should work with any Python ≥ 2.6 (including Python 3), as well as PyPy.

So I figured I'd let you know about these seemingly breaking changes.

issue_title: Add a proto equivalent to sc2_typeenums.h
issue_body:
https://github.com/Blizzard/s2client-api/blob/master/include/sc2api/sc2_typeenums.h

As sc2_typeeenums.h says, "All units and abilities are represented as unsigned numbers in the StarCraft II engine. This can make issue abilities unnecessarily hard."

This fact is also true with the proto API. As it is, any equivalent implementation of the sc2api client in another language will have to define its own equivalent of these enums, or force its users to figure out the unit indexes themselves.

Is it possible to define this as a protobuf enum as part of the protocol sent by the game?

```
zones = list(soco.discover())
for zone in zones:

		try: zone.pause() # can not pause TV, so could throw off an error

issue_title: error: Object created by ray.put could not be reconstructed.
issue_body:
### System information
- **OS Platform and Distribution (e.g., Linux Ubuntu 16.04)**:
- **Ray installed from (source or binary)**: binary
- **Ray version**: 0.5
- **Python version**: 3.6


### Describe the problem
I first applied the Ray to my project. The following error occurred and I don't know how to solve it. 

### Source code / logs
```python
import ray
ray.init(object_store_memory=1000) # only allows one int
a = ray.put(1)
ray.worker.global_worker.plasma_client.evict(516) # should return 516
ray.get(a)
```

Here is the result printed:
`The task with ID 4a82e1d5445d3c9cc0b5ebb9640218e6f7db2ab3 is a driver task and so the object created by ray.put could not be reconstructed. 
Suppressing duplicate error message.

issue_title: Compilation error on CentOS.
issue_body:
Compilation on the Amazon Linux AMI currently fails with errors of the form.

```
In file included from /home/ec2-user/ray/src/common/state/table.h:7:0,
                 from /home/ec2-user/ray/src/common/state/table.cc:1:
/home/ec2-user/ray/src/common/state/table.cc: In function ‘int64_t table_timeout_handler(event_loop*, int64_t, void*)’:
/home/ec2-user/ray/src/common/state/table.cc:90:49: error: expected ‘)’ before PRId64’
     LOG_WARN("Table command %s with timer ID %" PRId64 " failed",
                                                 ^
/home/ec2-user/ray/src/common/cmake/../common.h:75:37: note: in definition of macro ‘LOG_WARN’
   fprintf(stderr, "[WARN] (%s:%d) " M "\n", __FILE__, __LINE__, ##__VA_ARGS__)
                                     ^
```

Replacing the instances of PRId64 with `%lld` or something like that (or just removing them) makes compilation work, although that probably isn't exactly the right solution (because it will introduce compiler warnings at least on Ubuntu).

Note that to install the Ray dependencies, I did the following.

```bash
sudo yum update
sudo yum install -y git cmake autoconf curl libtool boost-devel boost-devel-static unzip
sudo yum install -y gcc gcc-c++ make openssl-devel

issue_title: Excessive logging in Redis server during actor reconstruction.
issue_body:
Consider the following script.

```python
import ray
import time

ray.worker._init(start_ray_local=True, num_local_schedulers=2, num_workers=0, redirect_output=True)

@ray.remote
class Foo(object):
    def __init__(self):
        pass
    def plasma(self):
        return ray.worker.global_worker.plasma_client.store_socket_name


local_plasma = ray.worker.global_worker.plasma_client.store_socket_name

# Create an actor on a different local scheduler.
a = Foo.remote()
while ray.get(a.plasma.remote()) == local_plasma:
    a = Foo.remote()

# Kill the local scheduler that the actor is on.
ray.services.all_processes[ray.services.PROCESS_TYPE_LOCAL_SCHEDULER][1].kill()

time.sleep(1)

# Schedule a task on the actor and get the result.
ray.get(a.plasma.remote())
```

The script runs fine (it takes ten seconds for the monitor to detect that the local scheduler has died). But one of the Redis shards will continuously log the following message (you can see this more vividly if you omit the `redirect_output=True`).

```
WARN] (/Users/rkn/Workspace/ray/src/common/redis_module/ray_redis_module.cc:977) No subscribers received this publish. This most likely means that either the intended recipient has not subscribed yet or that the pubsub connection to the intended recipient has been broken.
```

The problem is that the initial publish (when the task is submitted) is not received by the dead local scheduler, and so the same publish is reattempted **infinitely**.

The retrying is done [here](https://github.com/ray-project/ray/blob/37282330c0ea687fd1b983176dce85731fcf189d/src/common/state/redis.cc#L884) and [here](https://github.com/ray-project/ray/blob/37282330c0ea687fd1b983176dce85731fcf189d/src/common/state/redis.cc#L952).

The logging is done [here](https://github.com/ray-project/ray/blob/37282330c0ea687fd1b983176dce85731fcf189d/src/common/redis_module/ray_redis_module.cc#L974).

issue_body:
I'm testing this on the default Amazon Linux AMI.

First I had to do `pip install cmake` to get a sufficiently recent version of cmake. Then I ran into this issue.

```
-- Checking for module 'arrow'
--   No package 'arrow' found
CMake Error at cmake_modules/FindArrow.cmake:119 (message):
  Could not find the Arrow library.  Looked for headers in , and for libs in
Call Stack (most recent call first):
  CMakeLists.txt:157 (find_package)
```

The issue seems to be that we hardcode the path `PKG_CONFIG_PATH=$ARROW_HOME/lib/pkgconfig` in `src/thirdparty/build_thirdparty.sh`, but on this machine it was generated as `$ARROW_HOME/lib64/pkgconfig`.

As a temporary solution, doing `cp -r $ARROW_HOME/lib64 $ARROW_HOME/lib` solved the problem.

issue_title: Add settings for mail_validation to allow for reusable and time-expiring codes
issue_body:
We have a number of apps deployed with PSA set up in email authentication mode. For the most part it works okay, but since the introduction of single-use code verification the process falls over for a small number of users: 

- User asks for a verification email to log in
- Verification email is sent to the user with a code
- Awful security appliance on user's network intercepts the email/the user's session and visits the link first, eating the session cookie
- Awful security appliance gets a 302 and hands that back to the user
- User gets told that their token has expired and to try again
- Repeat

To fix this, we've added two optional settings: 
- EMAIL_VALIDATION_ALLOW_REUSE (bool): validate_email() will not return False for verified codes
- EMAIL_VALIDATION_EXPIRED_THRESHOLD (int): validate_email() will return False if the code has a timestamp and is more than [int] seconds in the past

issue_title: Adding community guidelines or CoC
issue_body:
Hi all, I propose to add either a community guidelines statement or a code of conduct (CoC). Options:

- Community guidelines could be something like http://scikit-image.org/community_guidelines.html
- CoC could be something like http://www.numfocus.org/code-of-conduct.html

CoC is more common, but personally I have a preference for community guidelines. Here is a scikit-learn thread with rationale for why community guidelines instead of CoC that I agree with: https://groups.google.com/forum/#!msg/scikit-image/WFLLC0TyS8M/OtmA0xd1AwAJ

It's more important I think to add some welcoming statement then argue over what form exactly, so if @PyWavelets/core prefers CoC that's fine with me too.

issue_title: Cannot resolve js/function types in browser
Consider using similar timestamp policy to Zabbix sender
issue_body:
> Timestamps
> 
> If values are sent using zabbix_sender from a file with timestamps, then these timestamps will be adjusted to match server time. For instance, if an item's timestamp is “10:30:50”, the current time on zabbix_sender's machine is “10:40:03”, and the current time on Zabbix server's machine is “10:40:05”, then the item's value will be stored in the database with a timestamp of “10:30:52”.
> 
> Similarly, if a value is first sent to Zabbix proxy, which later sends it to Zabbix server, the timestamp will be first adjusted to match Zabbix proxy time, and then it will be adjusted to match Zabbix server time.

issue_title: sync --keepDays cannot handle out-of-order versions
issue_body:
When using `--keepDays`, commit 0cdddf67ef57c19d8e25e59e0970198e55ca87aa iterates over the existing versions with this assertion:

```
        # We assume that the versions are ordered by time, newest first.
        assert prev_age_days is None or prev_age_days <= age_days
```

However, that assertion may not be true if the previous versions were created with `--replaceNewer`; the timestamps may be in any order. You can see this with, for example:

```
# one version in the bucket
echo one >file
b2 sync ./ $bucket

# now the newer version has an older timestamp
echo two >file
touch -d 20160701 file
b2 sync --keepDays 180 --replaceNewer ./ $bucket
```

If you try to sync again, we hit the assertion:

```
$ b2 sync --keepDays 180 --replaceNewer ./ $bucket
Traceback (most recent call last):                                 
  File "/home/peff/local/bin/b2", line 9, in <module>
    load_entry_point('b2==0.6.5', 'console_scripts', 'b2')()
  File "build/bdist.linux-x86_64/egg/b2/console_tool.py", line 879, in main
  File "build/bdist.linux-x86_64/egg/b2/console_tool.py", line 806, in run_command
  File "build/bdist.linux-x86_64/egg/b2/console_tool.py", line 623, in run
  File "build/bdist.linux-x86_64/egg/b2/sync/sync.py", line 179, in sync_folders
  File "build/bdist.linux-x86_64/egg/b2/sync/sync.py", line 120, in make_folder_sync_actions
  File "build/bdist.linux-x86_64/egg/b2/sync/sync.py", line 85, in make_file_sync_actions
  File "build/bdist.linux-x86_64/egg/b2/sync/policy.py", line 99, in get_all_actions
  File "build/bdist.linux-x86_64/egg/b2/sync/policy.py", line 167, in _get_hide_delete_actions
  File "build/bdist.linux-x86_64/egg/b2/sync/policy.py", line 227, in make_b2_keep_days_actions
AssertionError
```

I don't see a quick fix here. The strategy used in `make_b2_keep_days_actions` does depend on traversing in reverse-version order, so sorting the list before the loop is out. I think it actually needs go back to handling the selective deletion of arbitrary versions from the list.

/cc @bwbeach

issue_title: Inconsistent representation of GridAsPoint return based on accept type
issue_body:
When making an NCSS Grid-as-Point request, the representation of the data on the python side changes based on the `accept` parameter. For example:

~~~
from siphon.catalog import TDSCatalog
from datetime import datetime
cat = TDSCatalog("http://thredds.ucar.edu/thredds/catalog/grib/NCEP/MRMS/Precip/catalog.xml")
fullDs = cat.datasets.get("Full Collection Dataset")
remote_access = fullDs.remote_access()
ncss = fullDs.subset()
query = ncss.query()
lat = 42.376 
lon = -82.9377
var = "RadarOnlyQPE01H_altitude_above_msl"

# no accept parameter
query.lonlat_point(lat = lat,lon = lon).time(datetime.now()).variables(var)
data = ncss.get_data(query)

# xml accept parameter
query.accept('xml')
data2 = ncss.get_data(query)

# csv accept parameter
query.accept('csv')
data3 = ncss.get_data(query)
~~~

no `accept` on `query` (TDS defaults to xml):
~~~
data 
{'RadarOnlyQPE01H_altitude_above_msl': array([ 0.]),
 'date': [datetime.datetime(2018, 2, 2, 10, 2, tzinfo=<siphon.http_util.UTC object at 0x1035d3470>)],
 'lat': array([ 42.376]),
 'lon': array([-82.9377]),
 'vertCoord': array([ 0.])}
~~~

`xml` (or `xml_file`) `accept` on `query` (should be exact same as above, and is):
~~~
data2
{'RadarOnlyQPE01H_altitude_above_msl': array([ 0.]),
 'date': [datetime.datetime(2018, 2, 2, 10, 2, tzinfo=<siphon.http_util.UTC object at 0x1035d3470>)],
 'lat': array([ 42.376]),
 'lon': array([-82.9377]),
 'vertCoord': array([ 0.])}
~~~

`csv` (or `csv_file`) `accept` on `query`:
~~~
data3
{'RadarOnlyQPE01H_altitude_above_msl': array(0.0),
 'latitude': array(42.376),
 'longitude': array(-82.9377),
 'time': array(b'2018-02-02T10:02:00Z',
       dtype='|S20'),
 'vertCoord': array(0.0)}
~~~

The differences I see are that one (`csv`) returns numpy scalar arrays for data and a numpy scalar string array for time, while the other (`xml`) returns numpy ndarrays for data and a `list` of `datetime` object(s) for time.

issue_title: Confusing validation error when submitting JSON in Django REST API format
issue_body:
Thanks for creating this project! I really like both Django REST Framework and the design of jsonapi.org, so being able to use them together is excellent.

However, with the current 2.0.0a2 release, I found an interesting problem with the interaction between DRFJA's validation errors, and DRF's presentation of those errors through the browsable API: https://github.com/tomchristie/django-rest-framework/issues/3446

What appears to be happening is that when a create request is submitted using DRF's default format (i.e. an attribute mapping at the top level of the JSON request), DRFJA is raising ValidationError with the details attribute set to a list, which DRF then attempts to treat as a mapping.

Assuming I've identified the origins of the error correctly, If the exception details stored a mapping instead, that could both report the field name that was expected, as well as avoid the secondary failure in DRF's error handling.

issue_title: Help on using logsumexp during SGD training
non-slow version of inputTensor
issue_body: Currently the `inputTensor` function is slow because it swaps the memory layout from numpy's row-major order to DyNet's column-major order. We are thinking of adding `inputTensorTranspose` that inputs a tensor and transposes its dimensions at the same time. This will allow for inputting numpy tensors without swapping the dimensions, which will probably be important when inputting data in this way.

issue_title: Use cookies
Support for Sketch
issue_body:
The application [Sketch](http://www.sketchapp.com) is a fairly popular vector application on the Mac. I hope that support could be added at some point. Just for documentary purposes, some Sketch-specific inclusions that would need to be scrubbed (I haven’t found a complete specification yet):
* Namespace: `xmlns:sketch="http://www.bohemiancoding.com/sketch/ns"`
* Header:
 * `<!-- Generator: Sketch X.Y.Z (01234) - http://www.bohemiancoding.com/sketch -->` (will be removed with `--enable-comment-stripping`)
 * `<title>[...]</title>` (title of the exported Sketch object) 
 * `<desc>Created with Sketch.</desc>`
* In Sketch you can create separate pages, which seem to be exported even though they have no purpose in an SVG file anyway. I generally remove it after using Scour and have not encountered any problems as of yet.
```
<g id="Page-1" stroke="none" stroke-width="1" fill="none" fill-rule="evenodd" sketch:type="MSPage">
    <g id="[same as <title>]" sketch:type="MSLayerGroup">
        [...]
    </g>
</g>
``` 
* Sketch-specific attributes:
 * `sketch:alignment` (for `<text>`, not supported natively)
 * `sketch:name` (only seen in `<mask>` so far)
 * `sketch:type` (values: `MSPage`, `MSShapeGroup`, `MSLayerGroup`, `MSTextLayer`)

issue_title: If a worker times out and the task is marked as "FAILED", the history still shows it as "RUNNING"
issue_body:
Steps:

* Start a task via command line that will take a while
* Wait a few seconds (so it registers with the scheduler), then kill the task (control+C)
* Wait 60 seconds
* Go to the scheduler UI/API - the task shows as "FAILED", and the logs show "INFO: Worker Worker(...) timed out (no contact for >=60.0s)"
* HOWEVER - if you go to /history, the status shows as "RUNNING" and is never updated
* Even if you restart luigid, the task will forever show as RUNNING in /history

Looks like the history feature is not properly being updated, even though the UI is.

issue_title: udp loss 100% & MTU Detect
issue_body:
服务端使用最新版
已经在多个线路都测试了。UDP打洞是Fullcone

UDP
XboxONE网络详细测试的延迟测试会100%丢包
测试是通过x1ds.xboxlive.com (UDP协议，3478端口好像是)
另外PS4 UDP虽然转发没问题，但是一些游戏搜人明显变慢了(The Last of Us)

MTU
我的网络为光纤，PPPoE拨号，所以MTU应该为1492
路由器MTU设置为1492
不使用代理的情况下是1480 For Xbox ONE
不使用代理的情况下是1500(自动) 手动1492也可以正常，低于1492会提示IP封包错误 For PS4

使用ShadowSocks的情况下
仅代理TCP
Xbox ONE 自动检测为1500
PS4 自动检测1500，手动1492也可正常

代理TCP & UDP
Xbox ONE 自动检测为1500
PS4必须设置MTU自动或者1500 否则会提示IP封包错误

问题1:UDP转发存在一些问题
问题2:MTU应该设置为多少？我VPS的MTU是1500没错。但我本地网络MTU应为1492，在这种情况下我的路由器下接入设备也应设置1492才对吧。

issue_title: 给ss添加了一个ss-tunnel的功能，用来直接转发dns请求（类似ss-libev的ss-tunnel）。
issue_body:
最近在研究怎么给ss-local添加一个类似ss-libev的acl过滤功能----即通过ss-local做基于ip的代理选择（而不是通过系统的pac）。但是研究来研究去，发现必须要添加一个类似ss-libev的ss-tunnel的功能，用直接来转发dns请求。虽然现有的 udprelay 可以转发dns请求，但需要通过socks5客户端才行。

所以花了一点时间对现有的udprelay做了一些改造，在请求数据部分增加了socks5 header，在接受数据部分删除了 socks5 header，最终让其可以直接转发dns请求。

默认在 127.0.0.1:53上启动服务，可通过下面的命令测试：
```
nslookup www.baidu.com 127.0.0.1
```

补充：

这只是一个初始版本，没有像 ss-libev 那样实现完整的端口转发，所以在端口不是53的情况下不能通过iptables把dns请求转发到tunnel。

已提交pr。

issue_title: feature: Row.allow_break_across_pages
issue_body:
I'll try to keep it sweet and simple... There is a variable, paragraph.paragraph_format.keep_together, when it's true it will keep the paragraph on a single page instead of splitting it if it's at the end of the page.

I tried using this in a table scenario and it didn't work, although Word has the capability. After comparing the document.xml between a document where a row can split pages and one that cannot, I found that it acts as a table row property instead of a paragraph property (cantSplit vs keepLines in XML).

I can't seem to find any info regarding this within python-docx so I may make my own docx function for it, but I've got very little XML knowledge so I'd like to avoid that.

References for @scanny if he sees this:
``` xml
<w:tr w:rsidR="00137A85" w:rsidTr="005B6BA3">
        <w:trPr>
          <w:cantSplit/>
        </w:trPr>
```
That's the XML with the row not splitting pages and this is without:
``` xml
<w:tr w:rsidR="00137A85">
```

Obvious difference is the fact there's no table row property section and also the initial table row definition is different. If I end up making my own XML edits for this I'll make sure to post them.

issue_title: Slow Rendering in pybullet
issue_body:
For RL I would like to have pybullet render as fast as possible. How would I best achieve this?
I tested the three connection modes with ER_BULLET_HARDWARE_OPENGL and got the following fps:

DIRECT +shadow 29 ( defaults to ER_TINY_RENDERER?)
DIRECT -shadow 47 (defaults to ER_TINY_RENDERER?)
SHARED_MEMORY +shadow  29 ( using App_PhysicsServer_SharedMemory)
SHARED_MEMORY -shadow   47 ( using App_PhysicsServer_SharedMemory)
(to test FPS times I used: https://gist.github.com/BlGene/9010fb3671dac3cc566ee659fcda155a)

The GUI or SHARED_MEMORY + App_ExampleBrowser connections run very slowly for me <10fps. This seems to work better others, a youtube video (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=__ilQzkPDNI)  mentions the ExampleBrowser running at 1000fps. 

The difference may be due to pybullet is selecting the wrong GPU on my machine (NVS 310 vs  GeForce GTX 1070).   Is there a way to control which GPU is used by pybullet?

Training for RL with the GUI connection would require the option not to open the ExampleBrowser window. I expected COV_ENABLE_GUI to do this. Would this be sufficient to allow running in headless mode?

The following line the documentation is confusing for me:
    "Note that DIRECT mode has no OpenGL, so it requires ER_TINY_RENDERER."
From this I understood that App_PhysicsServer_SharedMemory uses OpenGL, although its never explicitly stated. This does however not appear to be the cause as shown by fps performance and GPU memory consumption.

To get fast OpenGL based rendering another option would be to use the recently introduced dynamically loaded render plugins. One approach would be to adapt the roboschool rendering as a plugin. But before I attempt this I would like to ask if it is this a reasonable approach and if this code has already been written by somebody else.

Thanks for any help in advance.
